races.race_name: Adare Manor Opportunity Series Final Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Katie Daniels
runners.spotlight: Faded from two out in 2m7f at this venue in February; much improved in a first-time tongue-tie when a 33-1 winner of a 20-runner handicap at Fairyhouse; definite chance off 7lb higher.
runners.comment: Much improved in a first-time tongue-tie when a 33-1 winner at Fairyhouse, definite chance

runners.name: Long Branch
runners.spotlight: Best form has been in conditions races over longer distances than this; needs to find more on the evidence of a recent Wexford second; cheekpieces for the first time.
runners.comment: Best form has been in conditions races over longer distances than this, needs to find more

runners.name: Aurea Fortuna
runners.spotlight: Course 2m7f winner over fences; may have needed chase run early this month after a break; not without a chance despite a mark 6lb higher than his chase rating.
runners.comment: Course 2m7f winner over fences, may have needed chase run early this month after a break

runners.name: Straight John
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on debut at Leopardstown when 14l sixth behind talented stablemate Ballyfad; ran as well as could have been expected behind hot favourite Leader d'Allier (previously second to Ballyfad) over 2m at this venue; short-priced winner here in February; outpaced at Naas last time, longer trip should help his cause.
runners.comment: Course 2m maiden winner, outpaced at Naas last time, longer trip should help his cause

runners.name: Radiator Springs
runners.spotlight: Winning pointer; good run in third behind the useful Oldschool Outlaw at Naas in December before winning a maiden at Clonmel; backed  that up with a pleasing second in a Fairyhouse handicap, strong contender.
runners.comment: Backed up maiden win with a pleasing second in a Fairyhouse handicap, strong contender

runners.name: Cloonainra
runners.spotlight: Has done most of her racing over fences since a creditable fifth behind Thanksforthehelp in this race last season; led at the last then, faded in the closing stages; chase form disappointing since Listowel festival win.
runners.comment: Has done most of her racing over fences since a creditable fifth in this race last season

runners.name: El Champo
runners.spotlight: Winner of five races, up against it after failing to complete his last two starts; third reserve.
runners.comment: Third reserve, winner of five races, up against it after two failures to complete

runners.name: Mi Lucky Cailin
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; took a long time to open her hurdles account, winning in good style at Thurles in January on her 17th attempt; not a bad run in a Navan conditions race before putting a blot on her record when pulled up in Fairyhouse race won by Katie Daniels; first reserve.
runners.comment: First reserve, 1-19 hurdles record is a negative, in good form before Fairyhouse failure

runners.name: Jacovec Cavern
runners.spotlight: Winning chaser; weak record (0-18) as a hurdler makes it hard to be confident; not a big price when down the field at Gowran on latest.
runners.comment: Winning chaser, weak record (0-18) as a hurdler makes it hard to be confident

runners.name: Garrybello
runners.spotlight: Two hurdle wins came in France early in his career; placed on the Flat on sole outing in 2025; struggled over hurdles at Thurles in January.
runners.comment: Placed on the Flat on sole outing in 2025, struggled over hurdles at Thurles in January

runners.name: Sopelana
runners.spotlight: Has had a busy time since sent hurdling last August; three wins include two over 2m at this venue, including in a handicap with this rider aboard; form has been uneven; bounced back from a dismal Fairyhouse run when second at Navan, again for this rider.
runners.comment: Has won twice over 2m at this venue another good effort when second at Navan

runners.name: Better Times
runners.spotlight: Has been running well over longer distances than this; fourth off this mark at Clonmel last time after going up 6lb for a win at Downpatrick; rider has had an excellent season.
runners.comment: Has been running well over longer distances than this, rider has had an excellent season

runners.name: Claude
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner on good ground for Joseph O'Brien; well beaten in two outings for this stable.
runners.comment: Maiden winner on good ground for Joseph O'Brien, well beaten in two starts for this stable

runners.name: Kamino Bello
runners.spotlight: Wide-margin winner of a maiden at Sligo last May; possibly unsuited by winter ground in novice races at Fairyhouse and Cork;  jockey has enjoyed a fine campaign; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Wide-margin winner of a maiden last May, possibly unsuited by winter ground, not ruled out

runners.name: Kalanisi Flash
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner over 2m6f almost two years ago; has run only twice since then; poor effort over hurdles at Cheltenham in November after a satisfactory chase run at Downpatrick.
runners.comment: Maiden winner over 2m6f almost two years ago, has run only twice since, up against it

runners.name: Minella Supreme
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper last August (2m3f, good) and a Kilbeggan opportunity maiden hurdle in September (2m3f, good/yielding); nothing like as effective since then; improved ground and cheekpieces may be a help.
runners.comment: Won twice in the first half of the season, improved ground and cheekpieces may be a help

runners.name: Aguellid
runners.spotlight: French recruit, placed on his first three British starts before winning a 2m Wincanton maiden nine weeks ago; respected as one of two runners for the race sponsor who also has last year's winner Thanksforthehelp in the line-up.
runners.comment: Second twice before maiden win at Wincanton, respected as one of two runners for sponsor

runners.name: Minella Post
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a point and placed in two bumpers; solid efforts in defeat in maiden hurdles last season; has been off for a long time.
runners.comment: Placed in bumpers, ran in decent company in maiden hurdles, has been off for a long time

runners.name: Ryan's Son
runners.spotlight: Maiden winner on good ground at Roscommon last summer; lame on only subsequent start; relatively unexposed; hooded for the first time on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Maiden winner on good ground last summer, lame on only subsequent start, unexposed

runners.name: Moon Mission
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after nine attempts over hurdles; did well to finish third over 2m5f here on handicap debut in January; second reserve.
runners.comment: Second reserve, still a maiden, did well to finish third over 2m5f here on handicap debut
runners.owner: J T O'Connor & M P Gleeson & P M Fortune & A McGonigle

runners.name: Jolie Jewel
runners.spotlight: Consistent on good ground at around this time last year; less predictable later in the year; won on yielding-to-soft at Thurles in November; down the field at Leopardstown in December.
runners.comment: Consistent at around this time last year, less predictable later in the year

runners.name: Miss Manzor
runners.spotlight: Lacks the advantage of a recent run; makes stable debut; Straight John appears to be the yard's main hope.
runners.comment: Lacks the advantage of a recent run, Straight John appears to be the yards' main hope

runners.name: Thanksforthehelp
runners.spotlight: Out of luck since winning this race last year; only 3lb higher now, and has to be taken seriously; Rian Corcoran has enjoyed a successful breakthrough season in Britain and claims a useful 4lb.
runners.comment: Out of luck since winning this race last year, only 3lb higher, has to be taken seriously

runners.name: Toll Stone
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m6f on the Flat at the 2024 Galway festival; never landed a blow when 6-1 for the big amateur handicap at the same fixture last July; different proposition later that week, only just denied in a valuable 2m1f handicap hurdle; could be a big player if fit and ready.
runners.comment: Could be a big player if ready on his first run since going close at Galway last August

runners.name: Sir Allen
runners.spotlight: Overcame a long absence to win a 1m6f handicap at Navan a month ago; no success over hurdles since his juvenile season (course maiden winner) but managed a good third in a 2m4f Listed handicap at Naas last season; noteworthy runner for in-form stable.
runners.comment: Overcame long absence to win on the Flat last month, noteworthy runner for in-form stable

runners.name: Falcon Eight
runners.spotlight: Chester Cup winner in 2021, also a 2m Curragh Group 3 winner in 2023; seven Flat wins in all; at the veteran stage now; four seasons since his only hurdles success.
runners.comment: Seven Flat wins, at the veteran stage now, four seasons since his only hurdles success
runners.owner: Mark E Phelan & S P O'Connor

runners.name: God Of Thunder
runners.spotlight: Out of form on the Flat in recent months, poor form over hurdles since a maiden success at Limerick last June.
runners.comment: Out of form on the Flat in recent months, poor form over hurdles since a maiden success

runners.name: Hitak
runners.spotlight: Cork handicap hurdle win last season came at 3m (good); has been running over fences this season, overall form suggests a preference for further than this test.
runners.comment: Has been running over fences this season, overall form suggests a preference for further
runners.owner: Susan Cosgrove & Emer Bourke & Charmaine O'Neill

races.race_name: Connolly's RED MILLS Irish EBF Auction Hurdle Series Final

runners.name: Vitorio Piel
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner at last year's festival on yielding ground; off the mark over hurdles at fourth attempt when dropped back to 2m in a four-runner Leopardstown novice early last month, outpacing odds-on favourite Grey Jude from the last; beat all bar the unconsidered Sept Etoile in a 3m handicap at Fairyhouse; strong chance.
runners.comment: Course bumper winner, solid hurdles form including a handicap second, holds leading chance

runners.name: Simply Natural
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner; 0-4 in bumpers, best effort when beaten a short head at Naas in December, winner subsequently placed in a Grade 2 bumper at the DRF; runner-up to the experienced Eagle's Quest at Fairyhouse on hurdling debut; another solid display at the same venue last time.
runners.comment: Second to Eagles Quest at Fairyhouse, another solid display at the same venue last time

runners.name: Raise You Up
runners.spotlight: Backed up 2m3f maiden win at Limerick when accounting for Grey Jude and Dunleer Crystal over an extended 2m2f at Naas; outpaced over 2m at the same venue last time; this trip is probably closer to his optimum.
runners.comment: Beat Grey Jude and Dunleer Crystal over an extended 2m2f at Naas, 2m inadequate last time

runners.name: Dunleer Crystal
runners.spotlight: Well held by Raise You Up and Grey Jude on Naas running; has a good chance of reversing Wexford form with Ilallgoeswell and  De Jour En Jour, rider had to pull off an amazing stunt to keep the partnership when his saddle slipped after three out.
runners.comment: Held by Raise You Up and Grey Jude on Naas running, rider made stunning recovery last time

runners.name: L' Evangeliste
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point and bumper winner; second on hurdling debut at the Listowel festival; made all for 8l Galway maiden win in October (2m); jumped well when same tactics employed to win a rated event over an extended 2m4f at Fairyhouse in November; lack of a recent run is a mild concern.
runners.comment: Has given good front-running displays at 2m/2m4f, lack of a recent run is a mild concern

runners.name: Grey Jude
runners.spotlight: Useful placed form in bumpers; never worse than second in four runs over hurdles; quite closely matched with Raise You Up on Naas running; however, no match for Vitorio Piel at Leopardstown.
runners.comment: Closely matched with Raise You Up on Naas run, no match for Vitorio Piel at Leopardstown

runners.name: De Jour En Jour
runners.spotlight: Went close against Ifallgoeswell in a 2m4f maiden at Wexford; faces a tough task against winners here.
runners.comment: Second to Ifallgoeswell in a 2m4f maiden at Wexford, faces a tough task against winners

runners.name: Los Blanco
runners.spotlight: Consistent form before justifying favouritism in a Down Royal maiden in December; not as well suited by the race conditions as the other two on the same weight.
runners.comment: Not as well suited by the race conditions as the other two runners on the same weight.

runners.name: Eagle's Quest
runners.spotlight: Has run twice on the Flat since winning a maiden hurdle at Fairyhouse in February; has a slight edge over Ifallgoeswell on previous Gowran running.
runners.comment: Has a slight edge over Ifallgoeswell on Gowran running. two most recent run on the Flat
runners.owner: Jackie Cullen & A D Mullins & Ms Margaret Mullins

runners.name: Ifallgoeswell
runners.spotlight: Narrow winner of a 2m4f maiden at Wexford this month; that was his eighth start over hurdles, and his handicap rating of 116 was left unchanged; others are better suited by the race conditions.
runners.comment: Handicap rating of 116 left unchanged after maiden win at the eighth attempt, needs extra

runners.name: All Night Revival
runners.spotlight: Maiden is held by Eagle's Quest on Fairyhouse running and was behind three of these rivals at Wexford last time.
runners.comment: Held by Eagles Quest on Fairyhouse run, behind three of these rivals at Wexford last time

runners.name: The First Turn
runners.spotlight: Winning favourite on debut in a Thurles bumper last October; always behind in a winners' bumper at Leopardstown in December; no impression in the Wexford maiden won by Iflallgoeswell; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Bumper winner on debut, no impression in the Wexford maiden hurdle won by Iflallgoeswell

runners.name: Strange Currency
runners.spotlight: Form of his C&D third in February worked out very well (second, fourth and fifth all won next time); unplaced on next two starts, unseated in a handicap last time.
runners.comment: Fair third over C&D in February, unplaced on next two starts, unseated rider last time

runners.name: Green Angel
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden; third over 2m at Ayr last time; well below the standard likely to be needed here.
runners.comment: Still a maiden, third at Ayr last time; well below the standard likely to be needed here

runners.name: Green Chef
runners.spotlight: Has struggled in maiden hurdles at Thurles and Navan; out of his depth.
runners.comment: Has struggled in maiden hurdles at Thurles and Navan, out of his depth

races.race_name: Louis Fitzgerald Hotel Hurdle

runners.name: Riskaway
runners.spotlight: Beat Zeus Power (improved since) on hurdles debut at Thurles (2m4f, good to yielding) in November and couple of decent efforts on heavy; soon beaten however in 2m5f Grade 1 Turners Novices' at Cheltenham (Zeus Power third), but definite claims here down in grade.
runners.comment: Turner's also-ran but definite claims here down in grade

runners.name: Soir De Garde
runners.spotlight: Tramore maiden winner (yielding) last year; after a long layoff, was no match for stablemate El Fabiolo at Leopardstown but was running well considering he faced a stiff task at the weights when a faller at the last (held in third) behind two 150+ rated rivals over 2m at Cork over Easter; half-brother to State Man remains unexposed and could go well if staying this longer trip (Townend's choice); hood back on.
runners.comment: Cork run promising; 8yo remains unexposed and could go well if staying this longer trip

runners.name: Begorra Man
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced maiden has twice finished runner-up in 2m bumpers here and most recently again found one too good on returning to hurdles in 23-runner 2m Navan maiden last month, clear second behind an all-the-way winner (soft); brother won over 2m4f, so longer trip here could bring improvement.
runners.comment: Recent Navan maiden second a potential improver upped in trip

runners.name: Bunting
runners.spotlight: Modest strike-rate over hurdles for one of his ability, hasn't added to Limerick maiden win as a 3yo, albeit some good runs in top company; didn't land a blow in BetMGM Cup at Cheltenham on latest but previous Leopardstown fourth was a solid effort; major claims down in grade here.
runners.comment: Contesting hot handicaps of late; major claims down in grade here

runners.name: Mister Pessimistic
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten in first three starts, point-to-point last term and this season in Listowel bumper and 2m Cork maiden hurdle in November, making most; contested Leopardstown Grade 1 novices' both starts since, dropping away tamely before the last and then never sighted in first-time tongue-tie at the DRF on heavy; drops in class here and upped in trip.
runners.comment: Out of depth in Grade 1 novices' of late; drops in class here and upped in trip

runners.name: Quinta Do Lago
runners.spotlight: Made good start in the autumn, winning the first academy hurdle (Cork) and following up in 2m1f maiden here (heavy); better form on quicker ground when third in 2m Fairyhouse 4yo contest and when fifth of 22 in Fred Winter at Cheltenham last month in first-time cheekpieces; tries a new trip here and a contender if staying.
runners.comment: Fred Winter fifth tries a new trip here and a contender if staying

runners.name: Milan Forth
runners.spotlight: Former hunter chase winner; gained 2m4f handicap chase win at Cork in January but well held twice since in competitive 2m handicaps; belated hurdles debut here and likely tough enough task.
runners.comment: Fairly useful handicap chaser but tough ask on belated hurdles debut

runners.name: Tinto De Verano
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced maiden has shown mild promise in bumpers but remote third on recent hurdles debut at Wexford and looks a forlorn hope here.
runners.comment: Beaten a long way on recent hurdles debut at Wexford and looks a forlorn hope here

runners.name: Le Grand Peinture
runners.spotlight: Ran away with Navan (2m, soft) maiden on second start for this yard last season and solid enough effort in Listed handicap at Fairyhouse afterwards; back from 10 months' absence when pulled up in 2m Naas handicap in February, having raced prominently to three out; better for that run but fair bit to find on ratings here.
runners.comment: Better for comeback run but fair bit to find on ratings here

runners.name: Minella Boss
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on most recent of four attempts hurdling when making all in 2m Gowran maiden (heavy) last month in first-time tongue-tie, first two pulling well clear; had previously run well here on yielding; upped in trip now and good bit more likely needed.
runners.comment: Front-running Gowran maiden winner is upped in trip and good bit more likely needed

races.race_name: Channor Real Estate Group Novice Hurdle (Grade 1)

runners.name: Doctor Steinberg
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner has taken really well to hurdling, winning first three starts over a trip, notably Grade 2 Navan novice and Grade 1 Leopardstown contest (2m6f) at the DRF (beating Kazansky 8l) on heavy; much too keen and ultimately pulled up when favourite for Grade 1 Albert Bartlett on latest and hood now fitted; encounters much better ground now.
runners.comment: Albert Bartlett flop has to settle better and now hooded; quicker ground to contend with

runners.name: Zanoosh
runners.spotlight: Rapidly improving mare is seeking a six-timer, notable wins being 2m7f Grade 3 Limerick mares' novice and early this month in 2m4f Fairyhouse Grade 1 mares' novice, leading last; all wins have been on soft/heavy but gives the impression she'll handle this better ground and could have more to offer at this longer trip.
runners.comment: Seeking a six-timer and longer trip could bring more improvement; sex allowance crucial

runners.name: Fruit De Mer
runners.spotlight: Point-to-point winner landed odds on hurdles debut in 2m3f Naas maiden and has contested Grade 1 novices' since, plenty keen when third over 2m4f at same venue and much better form in 22-runner 3m Albert Bartlett, travelling much better and would have gone very close but for hanging left run-in when beaten 1.5l (soft); that probably wasn't the strongest renewal but improving 6yo has to be considered a major player here.
runners.comment: Albert Bartlett second may need to improve again to take this but quite possible

runners.name: The Passing Wife
runners.spotlight: Steadily progressive since upped in trip, winning 2m3f maiden here before considerably better form in 3m Albert Bartlett last month, only conceding second late on to Fruit De Mer having led at the last; handles this better ground and easier finish could suit.
runners.comment: Denied second by Fruit De Mer in Albert Bartlett; easier finish could suit

runners.name: Love Me Tender
runners.spotlight: Won a bumper, maiden hurdle (2m3f) and 2m Grade 3 novice without being extended, all on good-to-yielding; acquitted himself creditably in Grade 1 Leopardstown novices', including third to stablemate Doctor Steinberg (2m6f, heavy); most recently not at same level back in trip in Grade 2 at Fairyhouse; place claims at best.
runners.comment: Leopardstown third to Doctor Steinberg gives him place claims at best

runners.name: Spinningayarn
runners.spotlight: Point/bumper winner; won easily under no-nonsense rides from the front at Fairyhouse (2m4f, yielding) and here (2m3f, heavy) but met his match over 3m in Albert Bartlett when fifth (Fruit De Mer second, The Passing Wife third); clearly needs to find more.
runners.comment: Albert Bartlett fifth has ground to make up on both Fruit De Mer and The Passing Wife

runners.name: Kazansky
runners.spotlight: Nicely progressive on heavy ground over the winter, winning Limerick Grade 2 novice and chased home 8l winner Doctor Steinberg over 2m6f at the DRF at Leopardstown; didn't really land a blow (pulled up) in 3m Albert Bartlett on latest and others now preferred.
runners.comment: Didn't really land a blow (pulled up) in Albert Bartlett on latest and others preferred

runners.name: Generous Risk
runners.spotlight: Won point-to-point for Donnchadh Doyle last March before selling for £250,000; placed in three bumpers before successful hurdles debut in 2m5f Leopardstown maiden last month, making most and going clear from last (soft); no disgrace in chasing home a top-class novice at Fairyhouse (2m4f) last time, but more needed.
runners.comment: No disgrace in chasing home a top-class novice at Fairyhouse (2m4f)  but more needed

runners.name: Frankie John
runners.spotlight: Leopardstown maiden winner has contested Grade 1 novices all three starts since, notably remote fifth to Doctor Steinberg at same venue and better form on latest at Aintree in 3m1f Sefton Novices' when decent fourth in first-time cheekpieces (good-to-soft); this probably a stronger race so more needed.
runners.comment: Aintree fourth a career-best but probably a stronger race so more needed

runners.name: Le Frimeur
runners.spotlight: Won Nenagh maiden point-to-point last season and 2-2 over hurdles this campaign, in 2m4f Chepstow maiden (soft) and keen enough and novicey in jumping in 2m5f Newbury novices' last month (good-to-soft); big step up in grade but respected.
runners.comment: 2-2 over hurdles; big step up in grade but respected

runners.name: I Started A Joke
runners.spotlight: Won 2m maiden here in January (soft-to-heavy) and excellent run following month when favourite in very competitive 19-runner 2m Leopardstown handicap, going clear with winner from last; took another step forward when third to a top-class novice in 2m4f Grade 2 Fairyhouse novice on latest (Generous Risk second) in first-time cheekpieces, despite slipped saddle; good chance he'll improve again at 3m and don't rule out.
runners.comment: Did well considering saddle slipped at Fairyhouse; 3m could bring more

runners.name: Espresso Milan
runners.spotlight: Beaten in three bumpers for Fergal O'Brien but successful on opening two hurdle runs for Willie Mullins, in 2m maiden here and ready winner of 2m6f Thurles novice (yielding); didn't seem to get home in 3m Albert Bartlett, but worth this second chance at the trip and remains a potential improver.
runners.comment: Didn't seem to get home in Albert Bartlett but worth this second chance at the trip

runners.name: Dream On Mick
runners.spotlight: Bought for £42,000 in January after winning a Tinahely maiden point-to-point (soft) and successful debut for connections in 2m4f Carlisle maiden hurdle early this month, clear with a hot favourite going to the last (good-to-soft); type to relish 3m but this a huge jump in class.
runners.comment: Carlisle winner the type to relish 3m but this a huge jump in class

runners.name: Hipop De Loire
runners.spotlight: Classy sort on the Flat, third in last year's Doncaster Cup; 9yo has had just four runs over hurdles, winning Galway maiden last summer, but pulled up on both starts since last month's return, never featuring in Albert Bartlett and again in Grade 2 Fairyhouse novice (Generous Risk second); cheekpieces tried last time persisted with.
runners.comment: Talented Flat performer but yet to convince over hurdles

races.race_name: Race & Stay At Punchestown Champion I.N.H. Flat Race (Grade 1)

runners.name: Low Kick
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when third in a 17-runner bumper at Aintree last May for Jack Teal; bought for £170,000 at Goffs UK sale five days later; reappeared at Navan in January, chasing home odds-on winner The Irish Avatar (same ownership); showed the benefit of the run when a 10l winner at Naas in February; seems ground-versatile.
runners.comment: Stepped up from debut behind The Irish Avatar with 10l Naas win; seems ground-versatile

runners.name: The Mourne Rambler
runners.spotlight: Promising debut when second in a point at Portrush last October; made a good impression when landing a traditionally strong bumper at Leopardstown in December; thoroughly lived up to expectations when becoming the tenth consecutive Irish-trained winner of the Champion Bumper, staying on strongly after leading under Colin Keane a furlong down; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Sets a high standard with a handsome Cheltenham win, six of these rivals behind him then

runners.name: With Nolimit
runners.spotlight: Point winner last season, won five-runner Fairyhouse bumper in January; finished well when narrowly failing to reel in stablemate Broadway Ted in a Grade 2 at Leopardstown; finished in mid-division at Cheltenham.
runners.comment: Grade 2 runner-up after Fairyhouse win, ninth behind The Mourne Rambler at Cheltenham

runners.name: Boycetown
runners.spotlight: Bought for E120,000 after finishing second in a point; stepped up from C&D debut to record an emphatic win at Down Royal's Christmas meeting; had a tongue-tie added for the Grade 1 bumper at Cheltenham and reacted well to take fourth (second-best of the Irish) behind The Mourne Rambler; capable of making his presence felt.
runners.comment: Second-best of the Irish when fourth at Cheltenham; capable of making his presence felt

runners.name: Love Sign d'Aunou
runners.spotlight: Point winner for Ger Quinn at Loughanmore a year ago; bolted up by 24l from dual points winner Largy Star on heavy ground at Naas in January on debut for Willie Mullins; sent off joint-favourite at Cheltenham, failed to cope with ground officially described as good, and dropped away to finish 17l behind The Mourne Rambler.
runners.comment: Impressed on debut, better ground found him out at Cheltenham, rejected by Patrick Mullins

runners.name: Dromard
runners.spotlight: Dam French hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to Prix du Cadran winner and Ascot Gold Cup third, Le Miracle; runaway winner of a heavy-ground Gowran bumper on debut on the eve of the Cheltenham Festival; would not have been out of place in that event if he had made it to the track earlier; gets a vote of confidence from Patrick Mullins.
runners.comment: Impressed on heavy going on debut. Patrick Mullins picks him ahead of Cheltenham also-rans

runners.name: The Wager
runners.spotlight: Went close over C&D last season on his only start for Pat Doyle; battled well for a narrow success at Fairyhouse in January; emerged from the Champion Bumper with more credit than several shorter-priced stablemates; stayed on from an unpromising position to finish seventh, only a neck behind Quiryn, who was best of the Mullins squad.
runners.comment: Emerged from the Champion Bumper with more credit than several shorter-priced stablemates

runners.name: Barnahash Prince
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to bumper/2m4f hurdle winner Barnahash Primrose; dam bumper/2m6f Grade 3 hurdle winner; looked like a horse with a future over jumps when making a winning debut at Cork early in the month; faces a big step up in class now
runners.comment: Made a winning debut at Cork early in the month, faces a big step up in class now

runners.name: Of Land And Sea
runners.spotlight: Huge price at Cheltenham, lost all chance when the saddle slipped early; ridden by Patrick Mullins on all three Irish starts; enterprisingly-ridden very wide to win at Limerick.
runners.comment: Enterprisingly ridden very wide to win at Limerick, saddle slipped at Cheltenham

runners.name: The Irish Avatar
runners.spotlight: Changed hands for £200,000 after finishing second in a point for Colin Bowe last April; reappeared in January for an all-the-way 9l victory on soft-to-heavy at Navan, form franked by the runner-up Low Kick in winning by 10l at Naas; only fourth-best for the stable in an untypically tame challenge at Cheltenham; probably capable of much better.
runners.comment: Only fourth-best for the stable in an untypically tame challenge at Cheltenham

races.race_name: Colm Quinn BMW Handicap Chase (Grade 3)

runners.name: Dee Capo
runners.spotlight: Two chase wins including 2m6f handicap here in November (off 142) and at least as good form when staying-on fourth over same trip at the DRF; no match for stablemate Maxxum over 2m4f at Clonmel on latest; Jack Kennedy's pick.
runners.comment: 6lb higher than when winning here in November; more needed

runners.name: Down Memory Lane
runners.spotlight: Both chase wins have come at Navan, notably 2m5f Listed handicap in December off 144 (soft-to-heavy); Cheltenham run put a line through as badly hampered early on, but recent Grade 2 Fairyhouse effort was a lacklustre one; cheekpieces now reached for and joint top-weight has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Navan win was good but recent Fairyhouse run was poor; cheekpieces now tried

runners.name: Kilbarry Saint
runners.spotlight: Off the mark chasing on tenth attempt in 2m5f Clonmel mares' beginners last month (soft) but ultimately no match for a 10l winner nine days later in 2m5f Fairyhouse mares' handicap: front-runner should run her race but vulnerable.
runners.comment: Front-runner should run her race but vulnerable
runners.owner: Mrs Con O'Keeffe & Tadhg O'Sullivan & Anthony O'Keeffe

runners.name: King Alexander
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced novice landed odds in 2m4f Gowran beginners' in February and fine effort for one so inexperienced when fourth of 18 in 2m5f Jack Richards Novices' Handicap at Cheltenham on much better ground; more scope for improvement than most of these.
runners.comment: Cheltenham handicap debut fourth augurs well; ground fine

runners.name: Dreal Deal
runners.spotlight: Last win came at Killarney last summer but retains level of ability judged on recent Haydock veterans' handicap third (good), best run for some time; whether or not he can back that up against more progressive types is debatable.
runners.comment: Haydock veterans handicap third promising; whether or not he can back that up debatable
runners.owner: Mark Devlin & Philip Smith & Nigel O'Hare & P J Rothwell

runners.name: Karia Des Blaises
runners.spotlight: French hurdle winner; useful form in defeat over fences last season, off the mark when running away with 2m6f Thurles Listed mares' novice in February (soft-to-heavy); ran no sort of race in 3m5f Irish National latest, but not to be ruled out off a fair mark here.
runners.comment: Ran no sort of race in Irish National but not to be ruled out off a fair mark here

runners.name: Nouvotic
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced but already a better chaser than hurdler, two wins coming over 2m4f at Wexford, most recently showing good attitude back in October on good-to-yielding; absent since but conditions to suit and likely more to offer.
runners.comment: Absent since Wexford win in October off 122; conditions to suit and likely more to offer

runners.name: Riaan
runners.spotlight: Chase wins have been at Wexford and Sligo on decent ground, latter occasion when beating Jesse Evans 3.5l at levels in October when last seen, albeit steady pace possibly suited; has run well fresh and has his ground here, so one to consider.
runners.comment: Sligo winner has run well fresh and has his ground here so one to consider

runners.name: Midnight It Is
runners.spotlight: Hurdle winner at 2m3f but rarely ventures beyond 2m1f now, good third in last season's Grand Annual at Cheltenham but didn't seem to get home in 2m4f Plate at same venue last month; stamina concerns remain at this trip.
runners.comment: Didn't seem to get home in 2m4f Plate last month; stamina concerns remain at this trip

runners.name: Magic McColgan
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner here last season (2m4f, soft) is 0-5 chasing this term, standout run when only caught close home by a strong favourite in 2m4f Naas handicap in February (heavy), having hit the last; reportedly never travelled before pulled up over 3m at Navan last month, but claims if back to her Naas form.
runners.comment: Never travelled latest, previous Naas second would give her definite claims

runners.name: Jesse Evans
runners.spotlight: Versatile 10yo won Grade 3 over hurdles last summer before finishing fine second in 2m6f Galway Plate off 148; absent since runner-up over hurdles at Tipperary in October, so may need this although ground in his favour.
runners.comment: Absent since October so may need this although ground in his favour

runners.name: Conyers Hill
runners.spotlight: Novice win came at Down Royal last season and pick of this season's handicap form when fourth in 2m4f Paddy Power Gold Cup at Cheltenham and held narrow lead when faller two out in 2m4f Tim Duggan Memorial at Limerick over Christmas; didn't stay 3m1f in Thyestes following month and has had time off since; could go well.
runners.comment: Didn't stay 3m1f in Thyestes; has had time off since, could go well back to optimum trip

runners.name: Where's My Jet
runners.spotlight: Hurdles winner is 0-7 chasing, decent run in very valuable 2m6f Leopardstown Handicap at the DRF (soft) but never counted following month in Jack Richards Novices' Handicap at Cheltenham; others look more reliable.
runners.comment: Penultimate Leopardstown run was decent but others preferred

runners.name: Classic Getaway
runners.spotlight: Grade 2 Red Mills Chase winner at Gowran last season hasn't recaptured that level of form this term, although third in same event was a solid run; last of three in 3m Grade 3 Cork conditions event on latest, back to probably his optimum trip here so considered.
runners.comment: 3m too far last time, back to probably his optimum trip here so considered

races.race_name: Ladbrokes Punchestown Gold Cup (Chase) (Grade 1)

runners.name: Gaelic Warrior
runners.spotlight: Confirmed his staying power with Bowl win at Aintree last year; got his campaign off to a fine start with John Durkan victory from Fact To File over 2m4f here; third in a blanket finish in the King George before 5l second to a restored Fact To File at Leopardstown; travelled powerfully for stunningly impressive Gold Cup triumph to leap-frog Fact To File in the ratings.
runners.comment: Impressive Cheltenham Gold Cup winner, has a stronger Punchestown record than Fact To File

runners.name: Fact To File
runners.spotlight: 2024 Brown Advisory winner, 2025 Ryanair winner; won the 2024 John Durkan at this venue, had a hard race when narrowly beaten by Gaelic Warrior in this season's repeat bid; failed to do himself justice in the King George; back to best when readily beating Gaelic Warrior and Galopin Des Champs in the Irish Gold Cup; fascinating rematch now after bypassing Ryanair.
runners.comment: Avenged John Durkan loss when beating Gaelic Warrior at Leopardstown, intriguing rematch

runners.name: Inothewayurthinkin
runners.spotlight: Cheltenham Gold Cup winner last year; no sign of best form on his first three runs this term; repeated previous Cheltenham trend of leaving winter form behind to finish third in the Gold Cup, despite not jumping particularly well; 0-4 at this venue and was trounced by Gaelic Warrior here as a novice; will need to catch the big two on an off-day.
runners.comment: 2025 Cheltenham Gold Cup hero, looks safely held by Gaelic Warrior on last month's clash

runners.name: Champ Kiely
runners.spotlight: Won the Grade 1 novice here last year, beating stablemate Ballyburn; has experienced a torrid campaign after starting off with a second placing over 2m; remote seventh behind Fact To File in the Irish Gold Cup; not a bad effort in the Grand National; making up the numbers here.
runners.comment: Won the Grade 1 novice here last year, making up the numbers on this season's evidence

runners.name: Grangeclare West
runners.spotlight: Excellent third in last season's Grand National; set tough tasks in Grade 1s this season before dropped in class to beat Gerri Colombe in the Bobbyjo at Fairyhouse; unseated at the first when 9-1 for the Grand National; stable's third string now.
runners.comment: Third in the 2025 Grand National, exited at the first this year, stable's third string now

races.race_name: Weatherbys General Stud Book Irish EBF Mares INH Flat Race (Grade 3)

runners.name: Coole Cafe
runners.spotlight: Surprise winner of Navan academy hurdle (soft) in December and improved markedly from bumper debut for wins at Naas and Limerick (Listed) under hold-up rides in testing conditions with hood fitted; has a tidy turn of foot and quicker ground could see her in an even better light.
runners.comment: Quickened nicely for two wins; quicker ground could see her in an even better light

runners.name: Kiltybo
runners.spotlight: Brings good bit of experience to the table, having won mares' bumpers at Thurles and Naas under forcing tactics; seemingly exposed under similar tactics at Aintree recently, but would be a dangerous one to give a soft lead to.
runners.comment: Front-running wins at Thurles and Naas; exposed at Aintree so more needed

runners.name: Belladaball
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to bumper winner and smart hurdler/chaser Meetingofthewaters; some promise first two bumpers but much improved to land Fairyhouse mares' Listed event early this month, coming off strong pace to win going away from Wonderful Everyday and Some Pretender on yielding-to-soft; quicker ground here but definite player if handling it.
runners.comment: Came off strong pace for Fairyhouse win; quicker ground but definite player if handling it

runners.name: Even Tho
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to useful bumper and 2m4f-2m5f hurdle/chase winner The Enabler; fell when having the race at her mercy in Dromahane point-to-point in December and debuted for champion trainer with decisive Limerick bumper success last month (first two well clear); nice prospect who could well play a role if handling quicker conditions.
runners.comment: Decisive Limerick success; could well play a role if handling quicker conditions

runners.name: Wonderful Everyday
runners.spotlight: Did well to beat the geldings on debut in Goffs Defender Bumper at this meeting last year (yielding), but had run nowhere near that level on softer ground until much more encouraging effort at Fairyhouse early this month when staying-on second to Belladaball in mares' Listed contest (yielding-to-soft); same terms here so more needed, but better ground to suit.
runners.comment: Has 3l to find with Belladaball from Fairyhouse; better ground should suit

runners.name: Latosca Du Houx
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to useful 2m/2m1f hurdle winner After Rain and French bumper winner Ultramaille; dam French 2m2f chase winner; had Goulmichette back in third when making winning Ayr debut in February for Nicky Richards, readily picking up that free-running rival a furlong out, despite looking green; has since joined top yard and interesting addition to its very strong challenge here.
runners.comment: Ayr winner for Nicky Richards has since joined top yard; respected

runners.name: Glens Anthem
runners.spotlight: Harzand half-sister to bumper winners Shantou's Melody and Glens Lullaby; dam smart bumper (at this meeting) and 2m2f-2m6f hurdle winner, sister to bumper/2m hurdle winner Glens Harmony; created good impression on last month's Navan debut, patiently ridden and readily going away from favourite final furlong (soft); Patrick Mullins stays loyal and likely major player.
runners.comment: Impressed on recent Navan debut; Patrick Mullins stays loyal and likely major player

runners.name: Some Pretender
runners.spotlight: Had two subsequent winners behind when justifying favouritism at Galway on debut in August (2m1f, good) and solid runs in defeat in three outings since without really progressing, most recently making most when third to Belladaball (Wonderful Everyday second) in Fairyhouse mares' Listed contest early this month; looking exposed and hard to see her reversing that form on same terms.
runners.comment: Third to Belladaball at Fairyhouse; hard to see her reversing that form on same terms

runners.name: Tintara
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to winners, notably Tombstone (smart bumper and 2m-2m3f hurdler/chaser) and Tintangle (useful bumper and 2m-2m2f hurdler/chaser); fell in her first two point-to-points; bought for 55,000GBP after a second-place finish in a mares' maiden at Ballycrystal and only outpointed close home by Born Believing on Cork bumper debut early this month; this looks a lot stiffer task.
runners.comment: Outpointed late by Born Believing at Cork early this month; this a lot stiffer task

runners.name: Maximillian Lady
runners.spotlight: Useful level of form earlier in the season for Cormac Farrell, running away with Galway mares' event (good-to-yielding) and clear second to an odds-on shot in Gowran mares' Listed contest (soft); returns from 9 months' absence for new yard with hood fitted.
runners.comment: Galway winner returns from 9 months' absence for new yard with hood fitted

runners.name: Celestial Tune
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to bumper winner Katie Connell; debut runner-up at this meeting last year and has returned this season to win mares' Listed bumpers at Listowel and Cheltenham, each time made plenty of use of; too keen and pulled up in Grade 2 mares' event at the DRF and has been given a break since; not out of it if settling.
runners.comment: Too keen at the DRF; claims on wins at Listowel and Cheltenham though
runners.owner: John Francis Ryan & Ciaran John Mooney

runners.name: Born Believing
runners.spotlight: Sister to useful 2m3f-3m4f hurdle/chase winner Born Braver among others; won Belharbour mares' maiden point-to-point on debut in February and followed up early this month in Cork pointers' bumper, needing every yard to get up close home (yielding) to beat Tintara; will appreciate a truly-run race.
runners.comment: Got up close home at Cork; staying type will appreciate a truly-run race

runners.name: Al Arrivee
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to Cheltenham Gold Cup winner Al Boum Photo; runner-up on Galway debut last season and returned to win at Sligo last summer (good) before returning to Galway at the festival when finishing a clear second to the smart Murat; absent again since but clearly goes well fresh and top amateur rider booked.
runners.comment: Absent since Galway festival second last summer; top amateur rider booked

runners.name: Sapphire Jewel
runners.spotlight: Sister to bumper winner and decent 2m4f novice hurdle winner Frankie John; plenty keen on last month's debut when a remote third to Even Tho at Limerick; has ability but hard to see her reversing that form.
runners.comment: Remote third to Even Tho on Limerick debut; hard to see her reversing that form

runners.name: Goulmichette
runners.spotlight: Ran away with Quakerstown point-to-point last season before fetching 170,000euros at this meeting; reappeared when turned over at long-odds on in Ayr mares' bumper in February, not getting home after pulling herself into clear early lead (third to now stablemate Latosca Du Houx); given a break since and hood now fitted.
runners.comment: Too keen at Ayr, given a break since and hood now fitted

runners.name: Treadonmydreams
runners.spotlight: Dead-heated in a point in 2024, her sole start in that sphere; beaten 18l behind Kiltybo on bumper debut at Naas last month so lots to find; tongue-tie fitted.
runners.comment: Beaten 18l behind Kiltybo on bumper debut at Naas so lots to find; tongue-tie fitted

races.race_name: Racing Foundation Supports The NHC EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Innichen
runners.spotlight: Early February foal by Mehmas; £65,000 yearling; excuses when promising sixth at Bath on debut this month and stepped up when second at Newmarket (also 5f, good) a fortnight ago; the only one of these with experience and there should be better to come.
runners.comment: Excuses on debut and stepped up when second last time; can put experience to good use

runners.name: Black Cuillin
runners.spotlight: April foal by Far Above; fourth foal; dam 7f/1m winner (RPR 83), closely related to 1m winner Kelowna, out of 7.5f-8.5f Listed winning half-sister to Irish St Leger winner Sans Frontieres; quite encouraging she's hitting the track this early, albeit might be one for further down the line; market can guide.
runners.comment: April foal by Far Above; dam 7f/1m winner; family of Irish Leger winner; market can guide

runners.name: Or Another
runners.spotlight: January foal by Dark Angel; 180,000gns yearling; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Tactical (6f 2yo Group 2), Handcuffed (6f 2yo) and Works Of Art (Listed-placed 6f 2yo), out of Listed-placed 6f 2yo winner, is granddaughter to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner; that pedigree suggests he'll make his mark as a juvenile; much respected on this introduction for powerful yard.
runners.comment: January foal by Dark Angel; 180,000gns yearling; family of 2yo winners; respected on debut

runners.name: Spectacular Diver
runners.spotlight: April foal by Dandy Man; £10,000 yearling; third foal; half-brother to 5f winner Military Girl (including 2yo; RPR 78); dam 5.7f/6f winner (93), closely related to AW sprint winners Tilly Trotter and Dee Sprinter, out of useful 5f 2yo winning half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Green Door; speedy pedigree and yard won this last year with a subsequent Listed-winning filly, albeit she'd already had a run; betting should be informative ahead of this debut.
runners.comment: April foal by Dandy Man; £10,000 yearling; speedy pedigree for last year's winning yard

races.race_name: Sir Peter O'Sullevan Charitable Trust Supports NHC EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Infraad
runners.spotlight: Promising 2yo debut (1m, soft); beaten as a strong favourite at Nottingham on his reappearance (1m2f, good) three weeks ago, racing too freely; remains capable of better and he is a leading form contender.
runners.comment: Two promising placed efforts 166 days apart; can do better still; leading form claims

runners.name: Asia Force
runners.spotlight: Clear promise in two AW novice events over the winter (1m2f/1m3f, Tapeta); can do better but likely he will need to if he is to make a winning turf debut.
runners.comment: Promise in two AW runs this winter (1m2f-1m3f); open to improvement but probably needs it

runners.name: Night Wolf
runners.spotlight: Promising debut in a traditionally strong York maiden last August (7f, good to firm; trained by John and Sean Quinn); couldn't build on it on a slower surface at Redcar in October (stable debut); remains with potential and this trip should suit on pedigree.
runners.comment: Promising debut at York (7f, good to firm) before a lesser run on good to soft; unexposed

runners.name: Why Not Win
runners.spotlight: 12th foal; closely related to winners Akeed Mofeed (7f-1m2f including 2yo/Hong Kong Group 1; RPR 120) and Jordan Sport (6f-7f including UAE dirt Group 3; 116), half-brother to four more including Wonder Laish (11.6f-2m1f Flat, 100; useful 2m hurdler); dam unraced; has a fair standard to aim at on debut but can't be ignored.
runners.comment: Well-bred newcomer, closely related to two Group-race winners; good standard to aim at

runners.name: Sea Lantern
runners.spotlight: Ran twice over 1m4f at Roscommon for Jack Davison last summer, finishing third on debut (good) before seeing off 14 rivals on soft ground a fortnight later; can win more races for new connections (sold for 75,000gns last October) but he drops in trip on faster ground and has to concede chunks of weight to some promising 3yos so this might not be one of them.
runners.comment: 1m4f soft-ground win in Ireland when last seen; sold 75,000gns since; tough task today

runners.name: Leighton
runners.spotlight: Top-class middle-distance pedigree, by Frankel out of a Group 1 winner in these colours; 2-1 joint-favourite for his Kempton debut in November (1m) but ran green and couldn't threaten in a race won by a smart prospect; should be a different proposition this year now his stamina is being drawn out.
runners.comment: Top-class pedigree; should leave his 2yo effort (1m) well behind him over middle distances

races.race_name: NARS Supports The National Horseracing College Handicap

runners.name: Toomuchforme
runners.spotlight: Sprung a 33-1 surprise at Southwell last month (5f) but that has been followed by two disappointing runs; best turf form on good ground; others appeal more for win purposes.
runners.comment: 33-1 win at Southwell (5f) last month has been followed by two modest runs; opposable

runners.name: Keldeo
runners.spotlight: C&D winner 12 months ago (good ground); back to winning ways in a 6f handicap at Catterick three weeks ago, doing well from a wide stall; has landed the widest draw again today and that could find her out.
runners.comment: C&D winner last April; arrives on the back of a 6f Catterick win; widest stall not ideal
runners.owner: Ownaracehorse, O Pears and T&T Racing Ltd

runners.name: Alpine Girl
runners.spotlight: Five-time turf winner; only 2lb higher than for her Redcar win last summer (6f, good to firm) and her two AW runs this year aren't the ones to judge her on (blindfold slow to be removed last time); leading contender.
runners.comment: Return to turf in her favour and this mark is in range; one to be interested in

runners.name: Rock Of England
runners.spotlight: Stepped up on his low-key reappearance when winning a 5f handicap at Newcastle 16 days ago; this demands more up 4lb and he wasn't at his best in one C&D run last summer.
runners.comment: 5f AW win 16 days ago was a good effort; 4lb higher mark demands extra from him

runners.name: Invincible Crown
runners.spotlight: Seems happiest on AW nowadays; not at best at Wolverhampton when last seen in December; trainer may have stronger claims with Jojo Rabbit.
runners.comment: Perhaps better on AW than turf; trainer also runs Jojo Rabbit who makes more appeal
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing VIII & Mrs R Carr

runners.name: Jojo Rabbit
runners.spotlight: Made all to win over C&D on his penultimate start (good to soft; Class 6); not in the same form over 6f on AW 12 days ago but he is well drawn back at 5f and his rider's 3lb claim is a bonus; key player.
runners.comment: Made all for C&D win 3 weeks ago; below best on AW (6f) latest; can bounce back quickly
runners.owner: Grange Park Racing XIII & Ruth Carr

runners.name: General Assembly
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; two AW wins (5f) last autumn and he was in good form when last seen in February; not much wiggle room off this mark though and a lower draw would have been preferable too.
runners.comment: In good form on AW when last seen; not much wiggle room off current mark though

runners.name: Call To Action
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 5yo; revived by blinkers last summer, following his Redcar fourth with a cosy win at Ripon (5f, good) 296 days ago; this 3lb higher mark is unlikely to prove beyond him but he is taking on match-fit opposition today.
runners.comment: Revived by blinkers last summer, winning cosily at Ripon 296 days ago; revised mark fair
runners.owner: D Scott, Resdev, Capla Dev & L Westwood

races.race_name: Tuffa Boots Supports The National Horseracing College Handicap

runners.name: Walsingham
runners.spotlight: Completed a hat-trick last summer with the first two legs coming over C&D (good ground); promising third at Ripon on his seasonal return two weeks ago, keeping on well from 2f out having earlier clipped a rival's heels as the race was hotting up; still has more to offer and the drying ground is the only imponderable (unraced on faster than good).
runners.comment: C&D record of 311; encouraging reappearance at Ripon; untried on ground faster than good

runners.name: Leadenhall
runners.spotlight: Ran out the easy winner of the corresponding event (good ground) on his reappearance last April; also successful at Haydock in August and that win came off a 2lb higher mark; interesting on return from a six-month break.
runners.comment: Won this race after a break last year; on a good mark and more appealing than many

runners.name: Vincent Rocks
runners.spotlight: Won novice races at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) and Southwell (1m, AW) on his first two starts; flopped on handicap debut last September (1m2f, good to soft) and he was out the back in the Spring Mile on his reappearance 32 days ago; down in class but he needs the cheekpieces/faster ground to turn things around.
runners.comment: 2-2 in novice company but both handicap runs have resulted in heavy defeats; headgear now

runners.name: Sir Paul Ramsey
runners.spotlight: Two turf wins at around 1m on good ground last summer, the second of them coming off a 1lb higher mark; not far off his best form when fourth at Lingfield early this month but he has since finished a remote 11th at Musselburgh; not a solid option.
runners.comment: Dangerous on best form but has to rebound from a lacklustre run at Musselburgh 17 days ago

runners.name: Sailthisshipalone
runners.spotlight: Soft-ground win for Richard Fahey last November (1m); tongue tied for the first time when making a winning stable debut over C&D three weeks ago (good to soft); 3lb higher in a better race and going without cheekpieces today; handles good ground but perhaps wouldn't want it any faster.
runners.comment: C&D win on stable debut this month; up 3lb in a higher grade and with cheekpieces absent

runners.name: Garden Oasis
runners.spotlight: Veteran with 14 wins to his name, two of them coming over C&D; ended 2025 quietly and he finished behind a couple of today's rivals at Ripon on his reappearance 13 days ago; on a dangerous mark but he may need another run before he is of major interest.
runners.comment: Conditions won't faze him and on a dangerous mark; needs to leave recent return behind him

runners.name: On The River
runners.spotlight: Won last summer's Carlisle Bell off this mark (1m, soft); ran well when fifth in the Spring Mile at Doncaster on his return before finding only his stablemate Milteye too strong at Ripon 13 days ago (1m, good to soft; Walsingham back in third); fully effective over C&D but he appears to have a preference for slower than good now and drying conditions aren't sure to suit.
runners.comment: Two solid runs this year and fully effective over C&D; drying ground the concern this time

runners.name: Cosmos Raj
runners.spotlight: Three wins last May/July at about 1m on various ground and he's back down to his last winning mark; ended 2025 quietly and there's a suspicion he'll improve for this comeback run; withdrawn after getting upset in the stalls at Ripon two weeks ago (25-1 at the time).
runners.comment: Three wins last summer at around 1m; on last winning mark but has 208-day absence to defy

runners.name: Billyb
runners.spotlight: Added another two 1m wins to his CV in 2025, including on AW when last seen in November; this 2lb higher mark shouldn't prove beyond him but his record fresh, allied to the absence of cheekpieces (worn on last seven starts), suggest this may not be his day.
runners.comment: As good as ever last year, winning twice, but returns from an absence minus cheekpieces

races.race_name: HBLB Supports The National Horseracing College Handicap

runners.name: Modern Times
runners.spotlight: Ripon maiden win (6f, good) last June on his second start for Tim Easterby; shaped nicely on his return from ten months off back at Ripon two weeks ago, poorly positioned but running the fastest final 2f split to snatch a never-dangerous fifth; should be sharper today and he's interesting off the same mark.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely at Ripon on his return two weeks ago; leading claims if building on it

runners.name: The Good Biscuit
runners.spotlight: Two course wins last year, over 6f on good ground in July and 5f on soft in October; pleasing third at Catterick on his reappearance three weeks ago; should go well with Warren Fentiman taking off a useful 3lb.
runners.comment: Two course wins last year; promising return at Catterick; firmly in the mix

runners.name: Mystical Storm
runners.spotlight: Underwent wind surgery prior to his Doncaster novice success last July (6f, good); ran well in a 7f handicap at York two weeks later but his form dropped off afterwards; gelded during his absence; dangerous mark but may find others having his measure now back from a break.
runners.comment: 6f novice win last summer; ended 2025 quietly and others are preferred this time

runners.name: Sixtygeesbaby
runners.spotlight: Not at his best in two runs for David O'Meara but on good to soft ground; possible this return to a faster surface will help and he's undoubtedly well handicapped if it does; waters muddied slightly by the stable also having Veblen Good in the race.
runners.comment: Not fired on good to soft for new yard; faster ground can help and he is well treated now

runners.name: Alpha Magic
runners.spotlight: Disappointed at Ayr when last seen 282 days ago; he had looked promising beforehand though and still has potential; can go well fresh but he is held by Sixtygeesbaby on their Doncaster run 12 months ago.
runners.comment: Disappointing when last seen but he'd looked promising beforehand; best days still to come

runners.name: Veblen Good
runners.spotlight: AW winner (5f) for Karl Burke as a 2yo; ran well over C&D on last season's reappearance and his fourth in a big field at York on his handicap debut came off a 3lb higher mark; two subsequent efforts are excusable (Listed company and then 7f on slow ground); sold for 32,000gns in October; unexposed as a sprinter and now drops in class; stable also runs Sixtygeesbaby.
runners.comment: Unexposed in sprint handicaps; drops in class for stable debut; interesting contender

races.race_name: Chadwick Lawrence Supports National Horseracing College Handicap

runners.name: Heartened
runners.spotlight: One win from 17 attempts came over 1m2f (AW; acts on sound turf) in September; reappearance fourth in AW classified wasn't a bad effort in March and she likely didn't stay 1m4f last time; still has work to do from out of the handicap back on grass.
runners.comment: Trip an excuse last time but she's out of the handicap and has work to do

runners.name: Lightening Company
runners.spotlight: Four-time Flat winner, including C&D, who has been all or nothing since scoring over hurdles at Cartmel in July; three AW no-shows followed good second over C&D (soft; 40-1) in October but market support predicted a going day when scoring here (1m4f, good to soft) three weeks ago; might not want the ground to get too quick but otherwise a key player under 2lb nudge.
runners.comment: All of nothing of late but scored over 1m4f here latest; key player if ground is okay
runners.owner: Middleham Park Cxv, Lycett Rcg & Partner

runners.name: Bearwith
runners.spotlight: Second in the 2024 running of this; nine wins include five on turf, all around this trip on a sound surface; won at Newcastle (AW) in February and it was a fine effort from the widest stall when second of 14 at Nottingham (also 1m2f, good) 11 days ago; big shout.
runners.comment: Fine effort from wide stall when second (1m2f, good) last time; has form here; big player

runners.name: Dubawi Time
runners.spotlight: 1m4f AW handicap win for the Gosdens and doubled his tally in soft-ground handicap at Yarmouth (1m2f) for William Knight, both in 2024; couldn't recapture that form last year and was sold for 8,000gns in October; starts out for new yard from career-low mark so worth a check of the betting.
runners.comment: 0-8 last year but starts out for new yard from career-low mark; worthy of a market check

runners.name: Finlaggan
runners.spotlight: Midfield on sole start for Andre Fabre last April; not competitive in a maiden on AW and turf (1m/7f) this spring but bred to be much better than he's shown so far; market watch essential on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Uncompetitive in two maidens for this yard; market check essential for handicap debut

runners.name: Piecederesistance
runners.spotlight: Won in France in 2020 and scored over C&D in 2022 but has developed a rocky profile and was in poor form when last seen over hurdles in the autumn; has joined Martin Keighley (same owners) but watching brief advised on this comeback in a tongue-tie.
runners.comment: 2022 C&D win but was running poorly over hurdles when last seen; tongue-tie for yard debut

races.race_name: Yorkshire Society Supports The National Horseracing College Handicap

runners.name: Volenti
runners.spotlight: Last of his five wins came 15 months ago and he struggled after close fourth over C&D (good to firm) in August; falling mark a help if he can stage a revival after three-month break; market check advised.
runners.comment: Struggled after close fourth over C&D (g/f) in August; reduced mark and back from a break

runners.name: Al Baahy
runners.spotlight: Turf winner back in May 2023 who found classified company his thing when going back-to-back on AW (9.5f/1m2f) in January; forced out of that grade subsequently and has found handicaps a stretch on last three starts.
runners.comment: Turf winner; two AW classified wins in January; handicaps have been a struggle since

runners.name: How's The Guvnor
runners.spotlight: Won on turf as a 2yo for Clive Cox; dual AW winner (1m/7f) for this yard in January but poor last time when the race reportedly came too soon; has been freshened up and might bounce back.
runners.comment: Won twice (AW) in January; may have been freshened up by a break; not discounted

runners.name: Powerful Response
runners.spotlight: Two soft-ground turf wins, including one for this yard, but he acts on better ground and made the most of a good draw when close third of 15 at Nottingham (8.5f, good; Token Love seventh) in cheekpieces three weeks ago; that is just about the best recent form on offer and he's nicely berthed again; visor goes on and he's respected.
runners.comment: Close third of 15 latest; well drawn again and he's respected; first-time visor

runners.name: Boubyan
runners.spotlight: Made all on good to firm at Newbury (1m; first-time cheekpieces, kept since) in July and doubled his tally when picking up AW handicap (1m2f) in March; raced freely when underwhelming on last two starts but drops in grade and has already dipped below his last winning mark.
runners.comment: Turf winner but quiet on last two starts; mark is fine and he drops in grade

runners.name: Star Start
runners.spotlight: Six turf wins include one here (1m2f, good to firm) in August; had wind surgery before a fair comeback at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) in March and built on that when fifth of 14 at Nottingham (1m2f, good) 11 days ago; drop back in trip no reason to rule him out and he's respected.
runners.comment: Six wins include one here; has been building up in two starts this term; could be poised

runners.name: We've Got This
runners.spotlight: One win (7f, good to firm) from 11 starts but can generally be relied upon to run her race and it was another solid effort when second at Ayr (1m, soft) in September; ground/trip fine and market may reveal whether she's tuned up for this reappearance; big player if so.
runners.comment: 7f winner (stays 1m) who generally runs her race; player if tuned up for this comeback

runners.name: Token Love
runners.spotlight: Yet to score but he went close at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) in December and was only beaten 3l despite finishing in midfield at Nottingham (8.5f, good; Powerful Response third) on his return three weeks ago; shaped well that day (hit the front before fading) and he's a candidate to build on that despite being drawn widest.
runners.comment: Shaped nicely on his return when ultimately midfield; tricky draw but still of interest

runners.name: Rajawail
runners.spotlight: 23-race maiden who has been tried at all manner of different trips, including 1m6f last time; this is arguably her best one and the reapplied blinkers might be a positive but she'd still be a surprise winner.
runners.comment: 23-race maiden who would be a surprise winner

runners.name: Bun Bang Fai
runners.spotlight: Wasn't placed in seven starts around this trip for Archie Watson, including one go on turf; sold for 4,000gns in October; hood/tongue-tie deployed for this stable debut/reappearance and she's worth a market check.
runners.comment: 7-race maiden; 4,000gns buy in October; hood/tongue-tie on for stable debut/reappearance

races.race_name: Summer Social Events At Southwell Racecourse Handicap

runners.name: Dollar's Dream
runners.spotlight: Very pleasing second, behind one who readily followed up next time, over the extended 2m at Newcastle in January; passed up a good opportunity next time and looked awkward under pressure latest (both 1m6f here); others preferred.
runners.comment: Passed over, mainly because he looked awkward under pressure here latest

runners.name: Betelgeuse
runners.spotlight: Has form figures of 152, all over 1m6f here, since a gelding operation; routed eight rivals then proved bitterly disappointing 15 days later; back from over five months off with fine second a fortnight ago (pair clear; PB); major player if putting best foot forward.
runners.comment: Fine second a fortnight ago (pair clear; PB); major player if putting best foot forward

runners.name: Clansman
runners.spotlight: Possible front-runner; high marks for consistency this year and managed a win at Newcastle in March; Toby Moore got a good tune out of him here the following month; sound claims.
runners.comment: Possible front-runner; high marks for consistency this year; sound claims
runners.owner: Mrs C M Clarke, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Volcanic Rock
runners.spotlight: Sent off odds on when debuting here last November but could manage only third and has gone backwards in UAE since; enough to prove on this marked step up in trip sporting a tongue-tie for the first time, for all that Oisin Murphy has a remarkable record for the trainer in the last five years (22-49; +40.94).
runners.comment: Enough to prove on this marked step up in trip sporting a tongue-tie for the first time

runners.name: Westlain
runners.spotlight: Pick of Flat form when close third at Lingfield last June (11.6f; turf; Betelgeuse behind); won twice over hurdles since and is now asked to go further back on the level; interesting candidate.
runners.comment: Won twice over hurdles and is now asked to go further back on the level; interesting

races.race_name: Book The Bistro In Sherwood Restaurant EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bellatina
runners.spotlight: Ran much better than market suggested she would over this trip at Beverley a fortnight ago, finding just one who had the benefit of experience too strong and finishing in front of a seemingly well-regarded Karl Burke/Amo newcomer who runs at Bath later today; obvious claims.
runners.comment: Obvious claims of going one better than on debut at Beverley a fortnight ago

runners.name: Sable Island
runners.spotlight: March foal; second foal out of 5f winner (including AW; RPR 76); trainer not noted for first-time-out 2yo winners; probably best watched.
runners.comment: Trainer not noted for first-time-out 2yo winners; probably best watched

runners.name: Crimson Blaze
runners.spotlight: Very green when managing fifth of eight on debut at Wolverhampton this month (5f; 11-2); the filly who finished just in front of her there scored next time; likely involved with Oisin Murphy booked.
runners.comment: Very green when fifth of eight on debut; likely involved with Oisin Murphy booked

runners.name: Estupenda
runners.spotlight: April foal; price rose from 26,000euros to 65,000gns as a yearling; second foal out of unraced sister to 1m2f winner Wondrous Words; local trainer yet to have a 2yo winner since starting up in Britain but market check wise.
runners.comment: Local trainer yet to have a 2yo winner since starting up in Britain but market check wise

runners.name: Frumoasa
runners.spotlight: January foal; 44,000gns yearling; third foal out of 6f winner (including 2yo Listed/AW; RPR 96); trainer had a 2yo win first time out under Paddy Bradley at Nottingham this month; watch market.
runners.comment: Trainer had a 2yo win first time out under Paddy Bradley at Nottingham this month

runners.name: Quiet Soul
runners.spotlight: March foal costing 15,000euros; seventh foal; half-sister to four winners, including Mamba Wamba (Listed-placed 5f/5.4f, including 2yo/USA AW; RPR 96); dam 6f 2yo/1m winner (76); first 2yo runner of the year for Michael Dods and makes some appeal.
runners.comment: First 2yo runner of the year for Michael Dods and makes some appeal

runners.name: Wopbopaloomop
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify favouritism on debut at Redcar last week (5f), not ideally placed but making a fair mid-race move before effort flattened out; tongue-tie goes on; close relation Candy improved with racing and ultimately won the Two Year Old Trophy at Redcar.
runners.comment: Much better expected here having been left poorly placed when sent off favourite on debut

runners.name: Havana Gift
runners.spotlight: Showed good speed before tiring when fourth of six on debut at Thirsk 11 days ago (5f; 12-1); no surprise if lasting longer with that run under her belt.
runners.comment: No surprise if lasting longer with debut run under her belt

races.race_name: Come Racing For Free 18th June Handicap

runners.name: Magician Of Riga
runners.spotlight: Pleasing third of five when stepped up to 1m4f at Wolverhampton last month and did the business under those conditions next time, getting the better of the hot favourite; had over 2l to spare at the line and a 5lb rise could have been worse; tough to beat.
runners.comment: Tough to beat given manner of breakthrough win; 5lb rise could have been worse

runners.name: Extraterrestrial
runners.spotlight: Twice third in handicap company at bit shorter than this, with the fitting of a visor not really helping at Nottingham three weeks ago (took good hold); less-severe headgear is reached for here; possibilities if settling better.
runners.comment: Twice third in handicap company at bit shorter than this; possibilities

runners.name: Singarda
runners.spotlight: Yet to make the frame but came closest to doing so on his return at Ripon 13 days ago (1m; following wind surgery); new trip raises a question.
runners.comment: Fair fifth on return but new trip raises a question
runners.owner: D Bishop, S Bland & T Coyle

runners.name: Al's River
runners.spotlight: Improved placing each start last year but, having been gelded, he returned with a low-key sixth on handicap debut at Nottingham three weeks ago (upped to 1m2f; 9-2; behind Extraterrestrial); rather watch.
runners.comment: Low-key sixth on return/handicap debut (upped to 1m2f; 9-2; behind Extraterrestrial)

runners.name: Flash Kozo
runners.spotlight: Better effort here 39 days ago despite still looking green; dam winning over 1m4f offers hope for this marked step up in trip; betting useful on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Better effort here 39 days ago despite still looking green; up in trip for handicap debut
runners.owner: D Bevan And Mrs D Bevan

runners.name: Wave Power
runners.spotlight: No forlorn hope on runs two and three last year, and moving up in trip promises to suit, but he's entitled to strip fitter for this return; gelded in his time off; AW/handicap debut.
runners.comment: Could win a race at about 1m3f but is entitled to strip fitter for this return

runners.name: Gemical
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in trio of Irish maidens at up to 8.3f on turf last year for Kevin Coleman; probably best watched unless the betting speaks positively.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in trio of Irish maidens at up to 8.3f on turf last year for Kevin Coleman
runners.owner: G C Gale & Stuart Brown & Diane Stewart

races.race_name: Celebrate With Sponsorship At Southwell Racecourse Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Dream Illusion
runners.spotlight: Three wins this year (two over C&D) and bounced back from disappointing Newcastle run with close sixth of eight over C&D this month, when the slow pace was against her; possible player.
runners.comment: Three wins this year (two over C&D) and undone by slow pace over C&D last time; a possible

runners.name: Donna Nook
runners.spotlight: 0-15 since winning in December 2024 on her second start but she ran a couple of reasonable races this January and returns from a break on another career-low mark; effective over C&D and might not be far away.
runners.comment: Her losing run continues but she has another career-low mark and might not be far away

runners.name: Wondrous Light
runners.spotlight: Having dropped back down to her last winning mark she's scored on her reappearance at Beverley (8.4f, turf) a fortnight ago; strong claims back up 3lb provided the return to Tapeta isn't an issue.
runners.comment: Won on recent reappearance at Beverley; strong claims if return to Tapeta isn't an issue

runners.name: Cosi Bear
runners.spotlight: Following a long layoff she's been down the field in a Kempton novice (7f, Polytrack) and a Lingfield handicap (1m, Polytrack) on her two runs this year; however, she remains very lightly raced and she showed plenty of promise here in October 2024; yard in fine form; no surprise to see a big run.
runners.comment: Hasn't shone this year but she's still very lightly raced and her yard is in fine form

runners.name: Welljudged
runners.spotlight: Disappointing at Wolverhampton (8.6f, Tapeta) in January on sole handicap start but she showed promise previously (including here) and 7f looked inadequate in a maiden at Chelmsford in February last time, when third; could play a leading role back up in trip and back in a handicap.
runners.comment: Disappointing on sole handicap start but has shown promise otherwise; a case can be made

runners.name: Electric Avenue
runners.spotlight: Won off 2lb higher at Newcastle (1m, Tapeta) last November but she's been a long way below that level on her last four starts.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb higher at Newcastle last November; long way below that level on last 4 starts

runners.name: Special Breeze
runners.spotlight: Ran okay when 5l fourth of five in C&D novice last September and it was her first run since when beaten only about 3l in sixth of ten on handicap debut at Beverley (8.4f, turf) a fortnight ago; her pedigree provides optimism that she'll have more to offer and she could be a contender.
runners.comment: Not beaten far on h'cap debut; pedigree provides optimism that she'll have more to offer
runners.owner: Michael H Sly Dr T Davies Mrs Pam Sly

runners.name: Brave Byreflection
runners.spotlight: She's below her last winning mark and may have needed the run at Kempton this month on her stable debut; however, her form was patchy last season and her claims aren't compelling.
runners.comment: Below last winning mark but has to raise game in a big way on second start of the season

runners.name: Alessia Fernanda
runners.spotlight: Her form went the wrong way last season but she returns having had wind surgery and is 7lb lower than when winning on her reappearance last April; trainer had a double here last Saturday; capable of having a big say.
runners.comment: Form went wrong way last season but she's had wind surgery and is on a dangerous mark

races.race_name: Dog Friendly Raceday Tues 2nd June Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Borderline Madness
runners.spotlight: Dual winner last year and matched best form when finding just one too good over C&D in November; recent return at Yarmouth should have blown the cobwebs away and a 1lb drop allows her into this 0-55.
runners.comment: Recent return should have blown the cobwebs away and a 1lb drop allows her into this 0-55

runners.name: The Spotlight Kid
runners.spotlight: Twice won back to back and has been knocking on the door of late, loudest when going down by a neck over C&D in January; player if he's not too fresh back from a short break and can be settled.
runners.comment: Player if he's not too fresh back from a short break and can be settled

runners.name: Ring Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Billy Garritty gets on well with him (form figures together read 421125) and the likes of Kessaar Power should ensure this is truly run, which will suit this hold-up performer; he does seem more effective over 7f, though.
runners.comment: Should get the pace he needs but does seem more effective over 7f

runners.name: Kessaar Power
runners.spotlight: Failed to beat a rival on last two outings but may have been rejuvenated by a short break and stall 1 is handy given he normally makes the running; Silvestre De Sousa is a positive booking too.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival the last twice but there are grounds for some optimism

runners.name: Kondratiev Wave
runners.spotlight: In good form over 7f of late, recording win number eight at Lingfield two starts ago (dead-heat); has the assistance of Oisin Murphy, but 1m around here just stretches him.
runners.comment: Has the assistance of Oisin Murphy; 1m around here just stretches him

runners.name: Starshot
runners.spotlight: 1-41; back-to-back good runs under Sean Kirrane in March but gave himself no chance of troubling the judge last week by failing to settle; usual tongue-tie returns.
runners.comment: Two good runs in March but failed to settle when down the field last week

runners.name: Mavetheforcebewivu
runners.spotlight: Should be all the better for her recent return to action but is yet to get within a length of winning and others are preferred.
runners.comment: Should be all the better for her recent return to action but others are preferred

runners.name: Pink Socks
