runners.spotlight: Legs two and three of her January hat-trick for Ian Williams came here (7f/1m); went off the boil for him and hard to fancy on what she's done in three starts for this yard.
runners.comment: January hat-trick but hard to fancy on recent evidence

runners.name: Vecchio
runners.spotlight: Mostly down the field for this yard, including over C&D last time; can't be supported.
runners.comment: Mostly down the field for this yard, including over C&D last time; can't be supported

runners.name: Manton Road
runners.spotlight: Regular slow starter; like The Spotlight Kid, his four wins have come in two lots of two; not beaten all that far in his two runs since leaving Gay Kelleway but more is needed from him.
runners.comment: Not beaten all that far in two runs since leaving Gay Kelleway but more is needed from him

runners.name: Shaws Phoenix
runners.spotlight: 0-15 and starts out for another new yard (trained last two years by Alice Haynes) off a 248-day break having undergone wind surgery; goes without headgear; likely best watched.
runners.comment: 0-15; likely best watched back from break and wind surgery for another new yard

races.race_name: Dog Friendly Raceday Tues 2nd June Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Timely Affair
runners.spotlight: Beaten favourite three times since breaking through over C&D in January but his last two efforts were sound; first-time headgear (cheekpieces) could help to focus him and Oisin Murphy is 9-28 (+12.68) for the yard in the last five years; the one to beat.
runners.comment: The one to beat under Oisin Murphy, who is 9-28 for the yard in the last five years

runners.name: Heretic
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back C&D wins last October (off 3lb lower than today, then 3lb higher than today); should appreciate returning to 1m having produced a lesser effort over 7f here last time; can be involved.
runners.comment: Back-to-back C&D wins last October; returns to favourite trip here

runners.name: Back From Dubai
runners.spotlight: Has won five times here; a line can be put through his most recent C&D run and he didn't get any peace out in front at Nottingham 11 days ago (weakened into eighth of ten); it wouldn't be the biggest surprise to see him bounce back (there is possible competition for the lead but no other out-and-out front-runners).
runners.comment: Could feasibly bounce back if left alone in front

runners.name: Ribston Pippin
runners.spotlight: Two wins in first season as a 3yo included a beating of the two clear market leaders plus Spirit Of Bowland over C&D in December; modest return to action under same conditions last month but could leave that behind today; Jason Hart, who gets aboard him for the first time, is 1-1 for the trainer.
runners.comment: Productive first season as a 3yo in 2025; could leave his modest return behind

runners.name: Cezarro
runners.spotlight: Left poorly placed when longer trip was revisited 32 days ago and looks best judged on good second at Kempton the time before (1m) on just his second start for this yard; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Looks best judged on good second at Kempton two starts ago; each-way claims

runners.name: Unknown Journey
runners.spotlight: Frustrating type (0-22 overall; 0-15 for Tony Carroll) and the fitting of a hood (retained here) saw no upturn at Kempton five weeks ago.
runners.comment: Frustrating type and the fitting of a hood (retained here) saw no upturn at Kempton latest

runners.name: Spirit Of Bowland
runners.spotlight: Readily landed a division of this race last year and sole win since came in C&D classified contest 34 days ago; should go well, for all that he might be vulnerable to younger legs.
runners.comment: Should go well, for all that he might be vulnerable to younger legs

runners.name: Haaf A Diamond
runners.spotlight: Pleasing third in first-time headgear (cheekpieces; retained) on her return at Nottingham 11 days ago; no good thing to build on that but yard had another big-priced winner recently.
runners.comment: No good thing to build on return third but yard had another big-priced winner recently
runners.owner: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya

runners.name: Pop Favorite
runners.spotlight: Hasn't troubled the judge since returning in December from a short break and finished well behind Spirit Of Bowland over C&D last time.
runners.comment: Out of form and finished well behind Spirit Of Bowland over C&D last time

runners.name: Wath Court
runners.spotlight: Placed once in 29 starts; no improvement for recent change of yard; best to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Placed once in 29 starts; no improvement for recent change of yard; best to look elsewhere

runners.name: Our Misty Blue
runners.spotlight: Yet to place, including when 33-1 or bigger in all four of his handicaps; first-time blinkers replace first-time cheekpieces; best watched.
runners.comment: Yet to place, including when 33-1 or bigger in all four of his handicaps

races.race_name: Conference & Events At Southwell Racecourse Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gold Star Gazing
runners.spotlight: Again third, but good step forward in RPR terms, when kept to 7f at Kempton in November; the winner was making it 2-2 for Godolphin and the second won next time; has undergone wind surgery since and tongue-tie is retained here; yards 3yos are going particularly well and this one could add to their tally.
runners.comment: The form pick and could be another 3yo winner for the Fanshawe team

runners.name: Wezzeer
runners.spotlight: 280,000gns yearling; learning on the job on debut at Kempton this month (6f; 9-2) before the penny dropped and he finished off well for third; looks set to take a good step forward and be even more heavily involved.
runners.comment: Eyecatcher on debut and looks set to be even more heavily involved here

runners.name: Percy's Star
runners.spotlight: Finished a few lengths behind the fourth at Leicester 16 days ago (6f) but it was a pleasing start; likely one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Pleasing start at Leicester 16 days ago; likely one for further down the line

runners.name: Elite Approach
runners.spotlight: Bought out of James Owen's yard for 3,500gns in February; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Pure Angel (5f AW 2yo; RPR 92) and Almotahed (KSA 6f-1m); dam 7f winner (76); already gelded; likely best watched unless market vibes are positive.
runners.comment: Bought out of James Owen's yard for 3,500gns in February

runners.name: Sugar Bon Bon
runners.spotlight: Third foal; half-brother to 5f/6f winner for this yard Sugar Baby (including AW; RPR 79); dam also won for this yard (6f/7f, including AW; 62); already gelded; trainer not noted for first-time-out winners on the Flat.
runners.comment: Trainer not noted for first-time-out winners on the Flat

runners.name: Hugh
runners.spotlight: Much bigger price than third-placed Wezzeer on debut at Kempton 23 days ago (6f) but looked more streetwise than that rival, holding every chance over 1f out, before weakening into fourth; minor placing may be best hope here.
runners.comment: Place behind Wezzeer on Kempton debut and wasn't as eyecatching

runners.name: Agric Ranger
runners.spotlight: This will be his third run of the month and he was beaten around 9l at Pontefract and Ripon (both 6f); looks more of a handicap prospect; one of two runners for Tim Easterby.
runners.comment: Beaten around 9l at Pontefract and Ripon (both 6f) this month; handicaps await

runners.name: Forest Phoenix
runners.spotlight: Similar profile (and has achieved a similar amount, albeit over 7f) to stablemate Agric Ranger in that he's having another quick run and looks likely to be seen to better effect once handicapping.
runners.comment: Similar profile/chance to stablemate Agric Ranger

runners.name: Likeadyinwasp
runners.spotlight: Small step forward, in RPR terms, at Newcastle last week but is still hard to fancy; should get a handicap mark after this.
runners.comment: Small step forward, in RPR terms, at Newcastle last week but is still hard to fancy

runners.name: The Cyprus Pearl
runners.spotlight: 125-1 when down the field on debut at Newcastle in February (6f); sole filly and looks best watched.
runners.comment: 125-1 when down the field on debut at Newcastle; sole filly and looks best watched

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Handicap

runners.name: Neyva's Angel
runners.spotlight: Typically lost ground at the start over C&D 24 days ago but came through to win under Oisin Orr; there may be enough pace in here, with the likes of Shazani, to set it up for the closers but 5lb rise demands more.
runners.comment: Typically lost ground at the start over C&D latest but came through to win; up 5lb

runners.name: Tabby
runners.spotlight: Big run on stable/handicap debut (formerly trained by Denis Quinn) at Wolverhampton last week (6f) when returning from over 15 months off; will take plenty of beating off the same mark if this doesn't come too soon; Hector Crouch is 10-46 (+11.32) for the yard in the last five years.
runners.comment: The one to beat if this doesn't come too soon

runners.name: Belsito
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins at Hamilton last September (6f); regular cheekpieces were left off for return and he shaped as if needing the run; can build on that in refitted headgear here; last AW run (over C&D) was his best effort on an artificial surface.
runners.comment: Much better can be expected here in refitted headgear

runners.name: Dicko The Legend
runners.spotlight: Triple AW winner who is just 1lb higher than when beating his 21 rivals at York last July (following wind surgery; winning the race for the second year in succession); completely blew the start at Wolverhampton last time and best watched today.
runners.comment: Completely blew the start at Wolverhampton last time and best watched today
runners.owner: Mrs S Hibbert, Foulrice Park Racing Ltd

runners.name: Fan Mail
runners.spotlight: C&D winner off 1lb higher last autumn; not beaten far at Newcastle of late, most recently when forced to challenge on the far flank; non-runner when due to wear headgear here last time; no aids this evening.
runners.comment: C&D winner off 1lb higher last autumn; not beaten far at Newcastle of late
runners.owner: L Bond/Sheikh Aymen Jawad Al Hamandi/Ptr

runners.name: Glamour Show
runners.spotlight: Outsider of the field both starts for Scott Dixon but didn't run like it, managing fourth then a front-running third; latest defeat was at the hands of the prolific Yorkshire Glory and Aisling Oscar; major claims on this drop in grade.
runners.comment: This 0-71 (effectively) is a big drop in grade; major claims

runners.name: Thurso
runners.spotlight: On the back foot early due to issues at the start a couple of times of late but has also disappointed; weighted to go well if Oliver Hoodless, who rode a 33-1 winner in February, can get a good tune out of him.
runners.comment: Well weighted but more miss than hit of late

runners.name: Shazani
runners.spotlight: Doubled his tally in Leger Legends race at Doncaster last September (comfortably made all at 40-1 despite going as far as 1m for the first/only time); just one run of note since but he did show more spark latest on his second run back from a break, in first-time blinkers (retained); not ruled out if taking to AW at the first attempt.
runners.comment: Showed more spark latest; not ruled out if taking to AW at the first attempt

races.race_name: EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (For Horses In Bands C And D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Hickory Lad
runners.spotlight: 18-1, shaped with plenty of promise when second to a short-priced Kevin Ryan runner at Thirsk (5f, good) 19 days ago, pulling well clear of the rest; will set the bar high for a race like this if making the anticipated improvement.
runners.comment: Sets a good standard on his promising debut second at Thirsk 19 days ago

runners.name: Kodi Bear Light
runners.spotlight: 28,000euros yearling; seventh foal; closely related to 5f/6f winner Sergeant Pep (including 2yo; RPR 93) and half-sister to winners Transparent (7f/1m AW; 91) and Lafan (1m/9.4f AW; 86); with a very powerful yard and a potential threat to Hickory Lad if the market signals are positive.
runners.comment: Newcomer from a powerful yard and the obvious danger to Hickory Lad

runners.name: Stay On Tab
runners.spotlight: 33-1, raced keenly just off the pace before fading into a well-held sixth on his soft-ground C&D debut 25 days ago; should stay in contention for longer if he settles better, particularly on this drier surface, but he'll need a big chunk of improvement to get heavily involved.
runners.comment: Too keen when well-held sixth on C&D debut

runners.name: State Of Gold
runners.spotlight: Reported to have lost her left-hind shoe when weakening to finish well held on last Monday's 5f Redcar debut (good to firm), with her betting prominence (9-2) suggesting better was anticipated.
runners.comment: Well held on recent debut but went off 9-2 so better was seemingly expected

races.race_name: Virgin Bet Supports Safe Gambling Handicap

runners.name: Fille Unique
runners.spotlight: 0-7 but last week's close third (Data Fata Secutus narrowly behind in fifth) of 11 at Newcastle (5f, AW) suggests she can win a race like this; showed promise on turf last summer; likely to go well.
runners.comment: Close third of 11 at Newcastle last week suggests she could win a race like this

runners.name: Pivotal Terms
runners.spotlight: Left her first three efforts for Iain Jardine behind when winning over 6f at Newcastle 16 days ago; the way she got on top towards the finish doesn't suggest she's crying out for this drop to 5f for the first time but the fact she's a last-time-out winner counts for something.
runners.comment: Won over 6f latest but fact she got on top late doesn't suggest she's crying out for 5f

runners.name: Data Fata Secutus
runners.spotlight: Pulled too hard in a pair 5f AW nurseries at Newcastle at the end of 2025 but settled better ridden with restraint on her recent reappearance there (5f again), keeping on to finish a close fifth of 11, with the reopposing Fille Unique third; entitled to come on for the run and should be on the premises.
runners.comment: Some promise when close fifth of 11 on last week's Newcastle return; in the mix again

runners.name: Riffa Spirit
runners.spotlight: Raced on AW, winning 6f Chelmsford novice for the Crisfords last summer; led until well inside the final furlong when fourth of seven in 5f Southwell handicap on reappearance for new yard 18 days ago; if he comes on only a little for that outing he could take a bit of pegging back in this lower grade.
runners.comment: Raced on AW, winning novice at two; fair fourth in handicap on return for new yard; claims

runners.name: Gillali Lass
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice in nurseries at Catterick (5f/6f) last summer; her C&D third on good ground towards the end of July was also creditable but she hasn't been seen since.
runners.comment: Placed in all three nurseries last summer but off since C&D third in July.

runners.name: Moretons
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden; not disgraced in 5f Catterick handicaps in recent weeks without suggesting a first win is imminent.
runners.comment: 0-11 and not showing enough to suggest that a first win is imminent

runners.name: Realistic Dream
runners.spotlight: Down the field in two 6f handicaps at Dundalk for Andrew Slattery this winter but her new stable is no stranger to success with recruits from Ireland, including with its other runner here; one to note in the betting.
runners.comment: Well held in two Dundalk handicaps this winter but needs betting check for new yard

races.race_name: Edinburgh Rugby Handicap

runners.name: Sycamore Gap
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on AW at Newcastle (1m4f) in September 2024 on his final start for Rebecca Menzies but hasn't seriously threatened in two recent AW outings for new yard since returning from a long absence.
runners.comment: Hasn't shown enough in two AW outings back from an absence to fancy his chances here

runners.name: Falaise Blanc
runners.spotlight: Came good off a similar mark at Newcastle (1m2f) last summer but well held in two outings there this year; his hopes are pinned on a change of headgear (first-time cheekpieces for blinkers) sparking a revival.
runners.comment: Won off similar mark on AW in 2025 but well held both starts this year; new headgear

runners.name: As Fast As Wind
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m6f at Salisbury (good to soft) for Daisy Hitchins last summer; has been running creditably in defeat over hurdles since joining Tristan Davidson, including placed twice here; leading claims back on the Flat in a race where her rivals have something to prove.
runners.comment: In good form over hurdles since joining this yard and bold show likely back on the Flat

runners.name: Star Of Markinch
runners.spotlight: Placed several times (including C&D) but this 10yo remains a longstanding maiden and failed to beat a rival on her Newcastle reappearance seven weeks ago.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D but is a longstanding maiden and failed to beat a rival on reappearance

runners.name: Glasses Up
runners.spotlight: Veteran who has won here but 11 of his 12 career successes having been gained at Ayr, including a hat-trick last spring; well handicapped on those exploits but has never won first time up.
runners.comment: Hit form last spring but has never won first time up in his long career

runners.name: Lieutenant Lily
runners.spotlight: Ten-race maiden; some promise on second start for current yard when sixth over 1m2f at Newcastle last month but well beaten over 1m3f at Southwell since; placed on turf last year.
runners.comment: Some promise for new yard when sixth at Newcastle but well held at Southwell since

runners.name: Cougar
runners.spotlight: Revitalised in blinkers and ended 2025 with victories over C&D (good to soft) and at Hamilton (1m4f, soft) in September; went without the blinkers when always behind on his Thirsk reappearance 11 days ago but could fare a lot better with the headgear back in place now.
runners.comment: Won twice in blinkers last autumn; no blinkers when well held on return but back on now

runners.name: Blondina
runners.spotlight: Modest maiden on the Flat in Ireland for Jim Bolger and has shown little in three hurdles for current yard this year; best watched back on the Flat unless the betting suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Modest maiden on Flat in Ireland and well held in three hurdles for current yard

runners.name: Gallus Norman
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two bumpers and three Flat runs at up to 1m3f last year but much more realistic chance now handicapping at a low level after nine months off; one to note in the betting.
runners.comment: Well held in bumpers and on Flat but more chance now handicapping; check betting

races.race_name: Hibernian Community Foundation Handicap

runners.name: Knicks
runners.spotlight: It's a long time since he won but he was a close third over C&D (good to soft) last September and was an encouraging sixth of 18 on reappearance at Thirsk (6f, good); refitted with cheekpieces (which are combined with his tongue-tie) for the first time since a win at Nottingham in autumn 2023; non-runner here on Sunday with a vet's certificate.
runners.comment: On losing run but reappearance encouraging and won the last time he wore cheekpieces

runners.name: Water Of Leith
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner, most recently on Wolverhampton AW last month; latest C&D sixth (Al Hussar third) of 15 wasn't one of his better efforts, though.
runners.comment: Prolific winner, most recently on AW last month; latest C&D sixth was below par, though
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Rosenpur
runners.spotlight: Five wins, all around a left-hand bend (5f-5.5f), in a productive 2025 campaign; his near miss on 6f Catterick reappearance (good) last Wednesday suggests he's unlikely to be long in opening his account for 2026; same mark today.
runners.comment: Had excellent 2025 and near miss on last week's return suggests 2026 can be productive too

runners.name: Al Hussar
runners.spotlight: Has returned with two solid efforts over C&D this month, latterly fourth of 17 to Montezuma (ran well again here on Sunday) on good to soft; likely to go well for in-form Grant Tuer.
runners.comment: Two solid efforts over C&D this month and likely to go well for in-form Grant Tuer.

runners.name: Jm Jhingree
runners.spotlight: Scored three times, including over C&D, during his spell with Jim Goldie but always behind on C&D stable debut 17 days ago; can only watch after that.
runners.comment: C&D winner for Jim Goldie but always behind on C&D yard debut 17 days ago

runners.name: Woohoo
runners.spotlight: Four sprint wins in 2025 (including C&D) but hardly shaped as if her next win was imminent when last of 12 on C&D reappearance 25 days ago.
runners.comment: Four wins last year (including C&D) but last of 12 on C&D return over Easter

runners.name: Sugar Hill Babe
runners.spotlight: Reappears off the mark she defied at Nottingham (5f, good to firm) last May and has won first time up before; also a creditable third on previous C&D visit; no surprise were she to go well.
runners.comment: Returns on a winning mark and has gone well when fresh before

runners.name: Curious Rover
runners.spotlight: Behind Al Hussar on his C&D reappearance 25 days ago but he did leave the impression the run would bring him on, weakening having led for a long way.
runners.comment: Behind Al Hussar on C&D return but entitled to strip fitter now
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

races.race_name: Virgin Bet A Good Bet Handicap

runners.name: Qazaq
runners.spotlight: Drawn a blank since winning first three starts for Roger Varian but surely would have put that right over C&D (good) last time with more luck in running, going down by only a head despite being repeatedly denied a run in the penultimate furlong; might need the breaks if he's dropped in from the outside stall but still has to be high on the list.
runners.comment: Looked unlucky when close second over C&D latest; big player if showing up in similar form

runners.name: Penny Mountain
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 17 starts but that came on New Year's Day at Newcastle (6f) and she's largely continued in form there since, placing over 7f on her last three visits; raced only on AW for this yard but her peak RPR was achieved on turf in Ireland.
runners.comment: In good form on AW at Newcastle, placing over 7f last three starts; player back on turf

runners.name: Tattie Bogle
runners.spotlight: Still seeking a first handicap success but he went desperately close at Kempton (7f) last month; sound return to turf when third of nine at Catterick (7f, good) last Wednesday, leading for a long way; respected at a track which suits prominent racers.
runners.comment: Led for long way when third at Catterick last week and can figure prominently again

runners.name: Counting Cards
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced 4yo who returned from an absence to finish runner-up off 2lb higher on his 1m Southwell handicap debut last October; too keen when failing to reproduce that form in two subsequent AW outings around the turn of the year but possible he could pack more punch at the finish if a first-time hood (combined with tongue-tie) helps him to settle better; runner-up on turf (good to firm) as a juvenile.
runners.comment: Took keen for own good on AW in the winter but claims if hood helps him to settle better

runners.name: Mission Command
runners.spotlight: It's now 15 starts since his last success but he comes here in good nick, finishing third on turf at Pontefract (1m, good to soft) and Leicester (7f, good) in recent weeks.
runners.comment: Now 15 runs since last win but arrives on the back of good thirds on turf in recent weeks

runners.name: Carolus Magnus
runners.spotlight: C&D winner (good) last October; also runner-up off today's mark back here in November and could play a prominent role again if fully primed after 134 days off.
runners.comment: Did well over C&D last autumn and claims if ready to roll after 134 days off

runners.name: Criminal Shore
runners.spotlight: Sole win was at Ripon (1m, good) last April but he's run plenty of good races since; last week's fifth of 14 at Newcastle (7f) was another respectable effort but he remains 2lb above the mark he defied at Ripon.
runners.comment: Drawn a blank since 1m Ripon win last April but several good efforts since

runners.name: Black Storm
runners.spotlight: 7f maiden win at Dundalk when trained by Kevin Coleman; placed on three of four starts for Katie Scott last August/September, including over C&D (good to soft); has had wind surgery ahead of this first start for seven months.
runners.comment: Promise for Katie Scott at end of last summer; off seven months (has had wind surgery)

runners.name: Giorgio M
runners.spotlight: Made all in 7f handicap at Lingfield off 2lb higher in December; some fair efforts in defeat since, including fourth of 11 at Wolverhampton (7f) four weeks ago; this former C&D scorer is handily drawn for a prominent racer; reasons to be positive with Jack Nicholls taking 5lb off.
runners.comment: 2lb below mark he defied on AW this winter; also former C&D scorer; reasons to be positive

runners.name: Catalyse
runners.spotlight: Never threatened when backed into 7-2 on his stable debut at Newcastle (6f) in January but remains one who could click for this yard and it will be interesting to see whether there is money for him again now back on turf after 95 days off.
runners.comment: Well held when backed on AW yard debut but remains one who could click for this stable

races.race_name: Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Alpine Sierra
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in AW handicaps on his last three starts, in the last two by narrow margins; equally as effective on turf, so has obvious claims for the track's top trainer.
runners.comment: Has gone close on AW on last three starts; obvious claims back on turf

runners.name: Esque Elegance
runners.spotlight: Finally got off the mark at 21st time of asking when seeing off 13 rivals over 1m at Newcastle (AW) under Conor Whiteley nine days ago; should be very competitive under a 5lb penalty if showing up in a similar mood.
runners.comment: Made it 21st time lucky on AW last week; claims under a penalty if in similar form

runners.name: Drumcondra
runners.spotlight: Runner-up twice at this venue (including this trip) last term; needs to be in better form than he was at Beverley last week (seventh of nine) but it's possible that refitted blinkers will give him a lift.
runners.comment: Twice second here last year but needs to shrug off a lesser run at Beverley six days ago

runners.name: Pallas Lord
runners.spotlight: Gained Newcastle win number ten in February; respectable sixth at Wolverhampton (8.6f) latest; it's hard to get excited by last year's turf exploits but he was runner-up over 1m here in April 2024.
runners.comment: Better known as an AW specialist but he has run well here before

runners.name: On The Bubble
runners.spotlight: 1-27 on the Flat; some of last year's best efforts came here but she's still opposable for win purposes after 190 days off.
runners.comment: Some fair efforts here in 2025 but 1-27 on Flat overall; off 190 days; others stronger

runners.name: Miss Raincloud
runners.spotlight: Exposed maiden who was placed over 7f/1m on AW last autumn but had lost her form completely when last seen in January; first-time cheekpieces (replacing hood) need to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Placed on AW last autumn but lost her way completely since; cheekpieces reached for

runners.name: Staffordshire
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over 1m2f at Newcastle in February, pulling clear with a next-time-out winner; didn't get the chance to show what he could do back there next time (saddle slipped); probably acts on turf.
runners.comment: Came good on AW in February; saddle slipped since, so that run can be ignored

runners.name: Come On John
runners.spotlight: Respectable fifth on AW last June on his first start for Alison Hamilton but lost his way badly in three subsequent outings; back from six months off and a leap of faith is needed.
runners.comment: Badly out of sorts when last seen in the autumn

runners.name: Spirit Catcher
runners.spotlight: Triple turf winner (7f/1m) but his last success was in August 2023 and he's been out of sorts since returning in September from a lengthy absence; mark is tumbling but he needs a major revival.
runners.comment: Out of sorts since returning from an absence; major revival needed

races.race_name: Livescore Bet Handicap

runners.name: War Memorial
runners.spotlight: Out the back on first four outings for Lawrence Mullaney but big step back in the right direction when second in 7f Southwell classified event seven weeks ago, pulling clear with one who has won and finished a close second in two handicap starts since; his last 11 outings have been on AW but was runner-up over C&D (good) in August 2024; bold show likely if a wide stall isn't too much of an inconvenience.
runners.comment: Major step back in right direction when second latest; draw not ideal but still big player

runners.name: The Gay Blade
runners.spotlight: Made it three wins in his last four starts (all 6f at Newcastle) when seeing off ten rivals in an apprentice race last week; former C&D scorer so the step back up in trip shouldn't be an issue and he escapes a penalty.
runners.comment: Has won three of last four on AW and escapes penalty for latest; C&D scorer

runners.name: Baby Rover
runners.spotlight: Won a 6f handicap at Redcar last summer; runner-up twice afterwards but didn't offer any obvious short-term encouragement on 5f Catterick reappearance three weeks ago; first 7f outing since finishing last on her course debut in 2024.
runners.comment: 6f win last summer but hard to draw any encouragement from recent reappearance
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And Partner

runners.name: Bernie The Bear
runners.spotlight: All three wins came on AW in 2023 when trained by Richard Fahey; only narrowly denied by The Gay Blade over 6f at Newcastle in February but hasn't been in same form since, including behind that reopposing rival there last week.
runners.comment: All wins came on AW in 2023 and recent efforts don't suggest he'll snap losing run now

runners.name: Filey Beach
runners.spotlight: Did well over C&D for Ruth Carr last autumn; below par in three outings since, although two of those were for another yard, and he was entitled to need his recent first outing back with Ruth Carr after five months off; a return to form wouldn't surprise.
runners.comment: May have needed recent reappearance and did well over C&D last autumn

runners.name: Classy Clarets
runners.spotlight: Back to form on AW for Iain Jardine in recent months, including 6f 0-50 classified win at Wolverhampton; found things happening too quickly for her over 5f in another classified event at Newcastle last time but kept on for a creditable third; 7f winner last year; one of two solid contenders for the yard.
runners.comment: In good form on AW in recent months, including 6f win; just as good at 7f

runners.name: Invincible Melody
runners.spotlight: Well held in 7f Thirsk handicap this month on his first start for Ewan Whillans, taking his record to 0-23.
runners.comment: Well held at Thirsk this month on first run for Ewan Whillans, taking record to 0-23

runners.name: Gimmieminnie
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; sold for 2,600gns to join current yard in January; fair effort when fourth of 11 in a classified on stable debut at Wolverhampton (6f) last month but well behind The Gay Blade at Newcastle last month; hood off, refitted tongue-tie and first-time cheekpieces combination on.
runners.comment: Fair start for new yard when fourth at Wolverhampton but well behind The Gay Blade since

runners.name: Without Delay
runners.spotlight: Favourably handicapped on last summer's form but hard to draw any positives from last week's Redcar reappearance after wind surgery.
runners.comment: Well treated on last summer's form but down the field on recent reappearance

runners.name: Approaching Dawn
runners.spotlight: Beat only one home in three qualifying runs over sprint trips last autumn but has a basement mark as a result and his half-brother Ahamonent is a fair handicapper over this trip, including a course win; hooded first time; one to monitor in the betting.
runners.comment: No obvious promise last year but has basement mark for handicaps and needs betting check
runners.owner: D P Van Der Hoeven & D G Pryde

runners.name: Due Respect
runners.spotlight: Came good in a first-time visor at Redcar (7f, good to soft) last July but the headgear didn't work as well in her three subsequent starts last year and is left off for her reappearance; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Came good in visor last July but out of sorts after; headgear left off on return

races.race_name: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Correspondence
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden and he didn't look an easy ride when second of four in a Wolverhampton novice in February, hanging badly right; only sixth of nine in a handicap at Newcastle (5f, AW) 73 days ago and now returns to turf on his first start since; others make more appeal.
runners.comment: 0-8 and didn't look an easy ride at Wolverhampton two starts back, hanging badly right

runners.name: Thecoffeepoddotco
runners.spotlight: Both of her wins have been over 5f at Lingfield (AW), the most recent coming in February off 2lb lower, and she ran well when third of seven there over 6f last month; respectable effort at Musselburgh (5f, soft) 25 days ago and has place claims back on quicker ground.
runners.comment: Both wins over 5f at Lingfield (AW); respectable effort on soft at Musselburgh latest

runners.name: Moe's Legacy
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D winner (good to firm/firm) who won on her reappearance at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) last month; bounced back from a lesser effort with a good run here over 5f on Monday; leading contender.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner who posted a good effort here over 5f on Monday; leading contender

runners.name: Savannah Smiles
runners.spotlight: Course winner over 5f on heavy ground in September and she has also won on good to firm; ended last season out of sorts and she was well held on her return at Leicester (6f, good) 16 days ago; hood is removed and tongue-tie is applied; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Course winner over 5f in September but struggled in four starts since; tongue-tie applied

runners.name: Addarella
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 6f at Wolverhampton (AW) when trained by Marco Botti and ran well when runner-up in a 0-90 there on her stable debut last month; however, she's yet to build on that in three starts since and is yet to prove as effective on turf.
runners.comment: Dual AW winner over 6f; yet to build on her good effort on stable debut; turf a concern

runners.name: Silver Wraith
runners.spotlight: Went close at Goodwood (6f, good to soft) in September and got off the mark at the ninth attempt when winning over this C&D (good to soft) in October; off since and now returns to quicker ground off this 3lb higher mark.
runners.comment: Off the mark at the ninth attempt when winning over C&D in October; returns off 3lb higher

races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Now EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Band D) (EBF Restricted Qualifier) (GBB)

runners.name: Lady Blanche
runners.spotlight: £18,000 yearling; showed plenty when a 1l third of 11 at Beverley (5f, good to soft; 13-8) on her debut two weeks ago; should have no issue with this slightly longer trip and looks the one to beat if coping with this quicker surface.
runners.comment: Showed plenty when a 1l third at Beverley (good to soft) on her debut; the one to beat

runners.name: Dowman
runners.spotlight: Dam 5f winner (RPR 79); showed plenty when second of ten here over 5f on his debut 24 days ago (third has won since); lively contender over this slightly longer trip.
runners.comment: Showed plenty when runner-up here over 5f on his debut 24 days ago; lively contender

runners.name: The Kalonji Man
runners.spotlight: Too green to do himself justice in the Brocklesby last month and he was beaten 11l when fourth of ten behind an easy winner at Musselburgh (5f, soft; well backed) 25 days ago; now encounters quicker ground and it will be interesting to see if his supporters return.
runners.comment: Too green on debut and beaten 11l at Musselburgh latest; now encounters a quicker surface

runners.name: Tina Fromtransport
runners.spotlight: April foal; 5,000gns yearling; third foal; dam unplaced 5f/6f AW (RPR 58), half-sister to dual 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Lusail, out of 6f 2yo winning half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Fayr Jag; dam's side of the pedigree is speedy and she's interesting on debut for a yard that runs two others.
runners.comment: 5,000gns yearling; out of a half-sister to dual 6f 2yo Group 2 winner Lusail; interesting

runners.name: Ariane Sky
runners.spotlight: March foal; 5,000gns yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to Polish 6f 2yo winner Kartacz; dam placed 12.5f AW (RPR 68), half-sister to winners Glorious Protector (11.5f Listed) and Full Moon Fever (11.6f/1m4f), out of half-sister to 1m4f/1m6f Listed winner Hasanka; the market should be informative.
runners.comment: 5,000gns yearling; half-sister to Polish 6f 2yo winner Kartacz; watch the market on debut

runners.name: Crystal Kraken
runners.spotlight: March foal; sixth foal; half-sister to winners Life On The Rocks (1m2f; RPR 81) and Laysoft (bumper); dam maiden (45) sister to Listed-placed 1m2f/1m3f winner Nibbling, out of 1m2f winning sister to 11.6f Listed winner/Derby runner-up Dragon Dancer; stable also runs C'Est Lui and Tina Fromtransport.
runners.comment: March foal; half-sister to winners Life On The Rocks (1m2f) and Laysoft (bumper)

runners.name: Holliesthedollie
runners.spotlight: April foal; 8,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-sister to 5f/5.6f winner Jojo Rabbit (including 2yo; RPR 92); dam unraced half-sister to German/Belgian 6f-7.5f winner Glueckskeks, out of 6f winning sister to 1m Listed winner Bomb Alaska; look out for market clues on her debut.
runners.comment: 8,000gns yearling; half-sister to 5f/5.6f winner Jojo Rabbit; look out for market clues

runners.name: Turtle Star
runners.spotlight: March foal; 8,000gns foal; second foal; dam unplaced 1m 2yo/9.5f AW (RPR 48), half-sister to Completion (useful 7f/1m), Bowling Russian (6f/6.5f) and Ernststavroblofeld (6f 2yo); yard also runs Holliesthedollie.
runners.comment: 8,000gns foal; second foal; dam unplaced 1m 2yo/9.5f AW; one of two for her stable.

runners.name: C'Est Lui
runners.spotlight: May foal; 4,000euros yearling, resold for 4,000gns; seventh foal; dam Italian 5f/5.5f 2yo winner (including Group 3; RPR 105), out of half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Miss Anabaa, is granddaughter of 6f winning half-sister to champion sprinter Owington; one of three for his stable; watch the betting on debut.
runners.comment: May foal; out of an Italian 5f/5.5f 2yo winner (inc Group 3; RPR 105); watch the betting

runners.name: Ricky Punting
runners.spotlight: April foal; 10,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to Italian 6f-7.5f winner Scapestrato (including 2yo); dam unraced half-sister to Italian 6f/7.5f 2yo Listed winner Mujadil Lachy, out of Italian Listed-placed 5f 2yo winning half-sister to two Italian sprint Group 3 winners; already gelded; yard's 2yos are worth monitoring.
runners.comment: April foal; 10,000euros yearling; half-brother to Italian 6f-7.5f winner Scapestrato

runners.name: Drum Major
runners.spotlight: Hung left and failed to beat a rival home on his debut at Newbury (5f, good; 40-1) 12 days ago; needs to take a big step forward.
runners.comment: Hung left and failed to beat a rival home on his debut at Newbury; needs big step forward

runners.comment: Has blown out in six starts in Hong Kong; opposable over the new trip

runners.name: Double Win
runners.spotlight: First and third in two starts over C&D; edging back down the weights and very much respected back under these conditions.
runners.comment: First and third in two starts over C&D; very much respected back under these conditions
runners.owner: Dr Kelvin Chong Man Yuk & Ma Yuen Yu

runners.name: Serangoon
runners.spotlight: Still 7lb higher than when winning at Sha Tin (1m1f) in February, but he does have winning form over C&D so is entitled to some respect on that alone.
runners.comment: Does have winning form over C&D so is entitled to some respect on that alone

races.race_name: Yeongcheon Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf) (Div II)

runners.name: Heroic Master
runners.spotlight: 2-40 in Hong Kong, both wins coming over 5f here; won over 6f in Australia, but out of the frame in all nine local starts over the trip.
runners.comment: 2-40 in Hong Kong; out of the frame in all nine local starts over 6f

runners.name: Awesome Treasure
runners.spotlight: Regressive in four starts for this yard, but has a better draw this time and is just 1lb higher than when successful over C&D in December; could go well.
runners.comment: Has a better draw this time and just 1lb higher than when successful over C&D in December

runners.name: Winning Now
runners.spotlight: Can lead; made the frame over C&D in November, but hasn't matched that form in four starts since; needs a resurgence.
runners.comment: Made the frame over C&D in November, but hasn't matched that form in four starts since

runners.name: Find My Love
runners.spotlight: C&D winner, but has only been successful in one of his last 34 starts and no real improvement since returning to Class 4 company; others appeal more.
runners.comment: C&D winner, but has only been successful in one of his last 34 starts; others appeal more
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Dannis Lee Cheuk Yin

runners.name: Brave Win
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all eight starts, though wasn't beaten far into fifth over C&D five weeks ago; will need to step up again.
runners.comment: 0-8; wasn't beaten far into fifth over C&D five weeks ago, but will need to step up again
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Matthew Lam Kin Hong

runners.name: Flash Star
runners.spotlight: Has had little luck with the draw in most of his starts since winning over C&D in December and that is also true today; hood removed.
runners.comment: Has had little luck with the draw since winning over C&D in December; that is true again

runners.name: Brownneedsfurther
runners.spotlight: Reserve; 0-9, but has been placed in all three starts over C&D, the latest when runner-up in first-time blinkers five weeks ago; races off the same mark so would hold each-way claims if getting in.
runners.comment: 0-9, but placed in all three starts over C&D; finished runner-up off this mark last time

runners.name: Ace Power
runners.spotlight: All three wins have come over C&D, the latest from 3lb lower last July; patchy form since, but he has the plum draw so a big performance would be no surprise.
runners.comment: All three wins have come over C&D; has the plum draw so a big run would be no surprise
runners.owner: Chan Ngan Chi & Chen Yung Hung

runners.name: Joy Capital
runners.spotlight: Something of a contrast in his five starts at Sha Tin (6f), his win in January rather standing out from his other four efforts; others have more convincing profiles.
runners.comment: Something of a contrast in five starts at Sha Tin; others have more convincing profiles
runners.owner: Jessica Man Yi Lee & Gregory Chi Ho Ho

runners.name: Bright Day
runners.spotlight: Sole win in Hong Kong came over 7f at Sha Tin in October, though having dropped back to the same mark he only went down by half a length to Smart Fighter over this C&D three weeks ago; back up 1lb, but a bigger problem may be the draw.
runners.comment: Only beaten half a length over C&D three weeks ago, but has a high draw to overcome

runners.name: Lightning Ace
runners.spotlight: Well beaten in two starts over 6f here and at Sha Tin; drawn wide so may be one for another day.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two starts over 6f here and at Sha Tin; drawn wide

runners.name: Starry Show
runners.spotlight: 1-3 in Australia, but little impact in eight local starts on turf/dirt; needs the switch to blinkers from cheekpieces to make a big difference.
runners.comment: Little impact in eight local starts; needs the switch to blinkers to make a big difference

runners.name: Smart Fighter
runners.spotlight: Reserve; three-time C&D winner, the latest when beating Bright Day by half a length three weeks ago; up 6lb, but would still make the shortlist if getting a run.
runners.comment: Three-time C&D winner, the latest three weeks ago; up 6lb, but still makes the shortlist
runners.owner: The Executors Of The Estate Of The Late Joseph Chung

runners.name: Crossborderdude
runners.spotlight: Has proved a model of consistency since December (331233), the last four runs under Zac Purton; proved he could handle this C&D when third three weeks ago and his reliability makes him a major player.
runners.comment: Has proved a model of consistency since December (331233); major player
runners.owner: Hung Sut Chung, Wu Haibo & Ken Hung Kam Lung

races.race_name: The Korea Racing Authority Trophy Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Quick Money
runners.spotlight: Won a 5f novice race just under a year ago but has found things tough in handicaps this season; difficult to enthuse over.
runners.comment: Has found things tough in handicaps this season; difficult to enthuse over

runners.name: Vigor Elleegant
runners.spotlight: Did well to finish seventh here last month, having lost significant ground at the start, but has largely been disappointing this season and is eligible for Class 5 races now.
runners.comment: Largely disappointing this season and eligible for lower-grade races now

runners.name: Sweet Briar
runners.spotlight: Ran well here on seasonal debut in November but seems badly to have lost his way and has double-digit draw today.
runners.comment: Has double-digit draw today and seems badly to have lost his way
runners.owner: Sammy To Ping Kwan & Cheng Shenn

runners.name: Gameplayer Elite
runners.spotlight: Gained breakthrough Hong Kong win over C&D last month, his third start for Douglas Whyte, and ran well in defeat here three weeks ago; has the inside draw again; major player.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month and runner-up here three weeks ago; well drawn again; big player
runners.owner: Lam Yau Pik, Rex Lam Lam & Monica Lam Mung Ni

runners.name: Legend Star
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner from off the pace this season; safely held off today's mark on latest outing six weeks ago, though, and far too inconsistent to rely upon.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season but on a tough mark now and not reliable

runners.name: Thunder Prince
runners.spotlight: Took 22 attempts to open his Hong Kong account but did it comfortably under Zac Purton here a week ago; 7lb higher and not so well drawn today but still features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Comfortable C&D winner last week; 7lb higher and drawn wider today; still respected
runners.owner: Wilson Chu Kwok Yiu, Paul M F Cheng Et Al

runners.name: Forza Leader
runners.spotlight: Unplaced all eight Hong Kong starts and recent efforts have been particularly uninspiring; significant improvement required.
runners.comment: Unplaced all eight Hong Kong starts and recent efforts have been particularly uninspiring

runners.name: Forerunner
runners.spotlight: Reserve; has modest 2-31 strike-rate but went very close over C&D last month and again ran well when fourth here three weeks ago; will have good each-way claims if he gets a run.
runners.comment: Has modest 2-31 strike-rate but ran well here on his last two appearances
runners.owner: Ng Tai Wing, Anson Or Yiu Wah & Henry Yeung Mun Kin

runners.name: King Cannon
runners.spotlight: Showed only minor promise on first two starts (6f/7f) and the addition of cheekpieces needs to prompt improvement.
runners.comment: Showed only minor promise on first two starts (6f/7f); cheekpieces added today

runners.name: Celestial Strider
runners.spotlight: Reserve; a down-the-field outsider on all four starts and seemingly on a beeline for Class 5.
runners.comment: A down-the-field outsider on all four starts and seemingly on a beeline for Class 5

runners.name: Origin Of Form
runners.spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; unplaced all five Hong Kong starts and seemingly in need of further help from the handicapper; tongue-tie added to headgear collection.
runners.comment: Unplaced all five HK runs and seemingly in need of further help from the handicapper

runners.name: Lucky Doctor
runners.spotlight: Won on soft ground in Australia last year but yet to make an impact after four Hong Kong runs; dropped in grade today but has bad draw; remains best watched.
runners.comment: Yet to make an impact in Hong Kong and has bad draw today
runners.owner: Dr Bosco Chan, Dr Chu Wai Ming Et Al

runners.name: Superb King
runners.spotlight: Did well to finish second from stall 12 over C&D last month and was also placed over 5f here three weeks ago; likely contender again.
runners.comment: Second from stall 12 over C&D last month and placed again over 5f since; likely contender

runners.name: Mighty Fighter
runners.spotlight: Has shown significant promise in trials but was always behind on last month's official debut at Sha Tin and is drawn wide here.
runners.comment: Promising in trials but did not feature on last month's official debut

races.race_name: Incheon Handicap (Class 4) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Another Zonda
runners.spotlight: Out-of-form 5yo who finished well down the field over C&D last month; lurks on a dangerous mark but needs to turn things around.
runners.comment: Lurks on a dangerous mark but needs to turn things around

runners.name: Romantic Laos
runners.spotlight: No longer the force of old, but has finished close up here on both starts (1m/1m1f) since dropped to this grade last month and seems likely to be on the premises again.
runners.comment: Not the force of old but close up on both starts since dropped to this grade last month

runners.name: Winning Data
runners.spotlight: Close third over C&D of today's mark last month but has rather mixed record this season and disappointed (albeit from wide stall) here three weeks ago; not the percentage call.
runners.comment: Placed over C&D two starts ago but has mixed record this season; not the percentage call
runners.owner: Dr Steven Li Siu Lung, Cheng Lap Yin Et Al

runners.name: Vivacious Win
runners.spotlight: In the frame over C&D on last four appearances, losing out only by a short head to all-the-way winner General Redwood on latest occasion; obvious contender.
runners.comment: Very close second over C&D this month, making the frame for fourth consecutive time

runners.name: Take Action
runners.spotlight: Reserve; slightly disappointing here a fortnight ago but came second on three of his four appearances before that and will enter calculations if he gets a run today.
runners.comment: Not at best here a fortnight ago but earlier form brings him into the equation

runners.name: Never Too Soon
runners.spotlight: Much better known as a sprinter but stays 1m perfectly well; twice safely held on dirt this month but ought to be happier back on turf and has slipped to a tempting mark; interesting.
runners.comment: Twice safely held on dirt this month but returns to turf on a tempting mark; interesting

runners.name: The Absolute
runners.spotlight: On a good mark now but yet to hit top gear after three runs this season, and both his wins were gained over 7f at Sha Tin; drawn wide here; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Yet to hit top gear after three runs this season and seems best over 7f at Sha Tin

runners.name: Justifying
runners.spotlight: 1-9, the win gained over 5f on debut last March; ran quite well when fourth on last month's dirt debut (6f) and has the inside draw for this first attempt at 1m; could have a say if stamina holds out.
runners.comment: Ran quite well on last month's dirt debut (6f); upped in trip for turf return; well drawn
runners.owner: Lau Sak Hong, Jimmy Wong Kwok Kin & Dr D L T Chak

runners.name: Excellent Boy
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; rallied well for second behind Beauty Viva over C&D two starts ago but was slightly below that form here three weeks ago; others appeal more.
runners.comment: 0-7; second over C&D last month but slightly below that form last time; others preferred

runners.name: To Infinity
runners.spotlight: Reserve; third over C&D in February but form has dipped again since, with refitted blinkers (retained) doing little to perk him up last time.
runners.comment: Third over C&D in February but form has dipped again since
runners.owner: Chan Wing Chiu & Yildiz Choi Yat Fan

runners.name: Lucky Together
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on recent dirt debut (6f) and has now been unplaced all six Hong Kong starts (6f-7f); sports first-time hood for this first attempt at 1m.
runners.comment: Unplaced all six Hong Kong starts (6f-7f); fitted with hood for first attempt at 1m

runners.name: General Redwood
runners.spotlight: Came good at fourth attempt, just holding on from Vivacious Win to make all over C&D three weeks ago; recent 6lb rise demands more from him but he has plenty of scope for further progress.
runners.comment: Just held on to make all over C&D this month, opening account at the fourth attempt

runners.name: Mighty Steed
runners.spotlight: Gave good account when runner-up over C&D three weeks ago and remains well handicapped on some of his early-season form in higher-grade company; respected, despite awkward draw.
runners.comment: Runner-up over C&D this month and still well handicapped on early-season form; respected

runners.name: Beauty Viva
runners.spotlight: Scored over C&D last month, opening his Hong Kong account at the 20th attempt, but was unable to land a telling blow off new mark here three weeks ago and needs the new cheekpieces to help him kick on again.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month, his 20th Hong Kong start, but safely held off new mark since

races.race_name: Seoul Handicap (Class 2) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Magic Control
runners.spotlight: Needs to up his game after some below-par efforts but is well handicapped on some of his early-season form and has the plum inside draw.
runners.comment: Needs to better recent efforts but is well handicapped and has the inside draw

runners.name: Bottomuptogether
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with some below-par efforts but returned from a break with a pretty good fourth at Sha Tin (6f) last month; also suited by this C&D and has become well handicapped; one to consider.
runners.comment: Ran quite well last month, after a break, and has become well handicapped; considered

runners.name: Son Pak Fu
runners.spotlight: Seven-time course winner over 6f but on the downgrade now; competes from 3lb out of the weights and has outside draw on this rare 5f start; not the most obvious answer.
runners.comment: Multiple 6f winner; on the downgrade now and has wide draw for rare 5f run

runners.name: Youthful Spirits
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in October and gave it a good shot from the front when close fifth over C&D last month, but faces tough task from 6lb out of the weights today.
runners.comment: Ran well when fifth over C&D last month but is 6lb wrong today

runners.name: Akashvani
runners.spotlight: Dominated from the front over 7f at Sha Tin in January but was safely held over that trip when back in a handicap this month and is 3lb out of the weights for this first 5f start; opposable.
runners.comment: Dominated over 7f in January but is 3lb wrong for this first 5f run; opposable

runners.name: Glowing Praises
runners.spotlight: Scored over 5f at Sha Tin on Hong Kong debut in February and was beaten only narrowly there on last two outings; 5lb wrong and not ideally drawn for first run here but he's not yet exposed in Hong Kong and Britney Wong claims 7lb.
runners.comment: Not ideally drawn here but has made a bright start to his Hong Kong career at Sha Tin

runners.name: Brave Star
runners.spotlight: Placed on three of his four 6f starts since winning over that trip here in November, but appears to have very little margin for error off his current mark and this drop back to 5f is not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Placed on last three 6f starts but this drop back in trip is not sure to suit
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Matthew Lam Kin Hong

runners.name: Candlelight Dinner
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner this season; arguably posted career-best effort when fourth from stall 10 here a week ago but is 7lb out of the handicap today and needs to find even more.
runners.comment: Commendable fourth from wide stall here last week but 7lb wrong today

runners.name: Colourful King
runners.spotlight: Still 11lb higher than for impressive win at Sha Tin (5f) in November but bumped into the much improved version of Stellar Express on his next two outings and has not enjoyed much luck over 6f since; still has a bigger run in him; respected.
runners.comment: Still 11lb higher than for impressive win in November but may yet have a bigger run in him

runners.name: Stellar Express
runners.spotlight: 6yo who has shown remarkable improvement over 5f at Sha Tin this year, with one of his three wins gained in a Group 3 handicap; on a tough mark and not ideally drawn but drops back to his ideal trip after two 6f runs and can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Very progressive over 5f at Sha Tin this year (three wins); can't be ruled out

races.race_name: Busan Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Spirit Of Peace
runners.spotlight: Usually finds the winning line coming too soon and has become frustrating; on the other hand, he scores good marks for his consistency over C&D this year and has a workable mark if he gets a strong pace to aim at.
runners.comment: Consistent this year but usually finds the winning line coming too soon; frustrating
runners.owner: Fong Chung Man, Vincent Fong & Lucia Fong So Ching

runners.name: Meowth
runners.spotlight: Placed on five of his six starts since Class 4 C&D win in November, the last two in this higher grade; quite nicely drawn today and ought to be right in the thick of things again.
runners.comment: Placed on five of his six starts since Class 4 C&D win in November; in the mix again

runners.name: Sports Legend
runners.spotlight: Still 9lb higher than for emphatic C&D win in December, but his fifth here a fortnight ago was perfectly respectable and he's better drawn today; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Sound effort when fifth here a fortnight ago and better drawn today; could have a say
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui

runners.name: Giant Ballon
runners.spotlight: Took form two new level when adopting front-running role last month, winning twice over C&D in good style; up 10lb since last time and moves up in grade too but could still take some catching.
runners.comment: Dominated from the front twice over C&D last month; upped in grade today
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs George Chang And George Hing Chang

runners.name: Ace Champion
runners.spotlight: Reserve; Ex-Irish 4yo; went close from the inside stall over C&D last month, his fourth Hong Kong start, and had the outside stall when only seventh here three weeks ago; will have possibilities if he gets a run.
runners.comment: Went close from stall 1 two runs ago and had wide draw when only seventh last time

runners.name: City Gold Banner
runners.spotlight: Reserve; scored on Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin (5f) in January, but two lesser efforts there have followed (5f/6f) and he now has a point to prove.
runners.comment: Won on Hong Kong debut in January but two lesser efforts have followed

runners.name: Flying Wrote
runners.spotlight: Just held on for narrow Class 4 C&D win in February and has performed well in this higher grade on all three starts since; better drawn than on last two outings; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Holding his own in this grade since Class 4 win in February; probably won't be far away
runners.owner: Simon Tsang Heung Tung & Shirley Tsang Huang Yang

runners.name: Copartner Fleet
runners.spotlight: Edged ahead close home to score over 5f here in January but safely held over 6f in this higher grade twice since; looks up against it again.
runners.comment: Twice safely held over 6f in this higher grade since narrow 5f course win in January

runners.name: Hey Bros
runners.spotlight: 2-7 in Australia; drawn on the inside today but has shown only minor promise in Hong Kong this year (three runs) and needs only to drop another 1lb to be eligible for a drop in grade.
runners.comment: 2-7 in Australia but has shown only minor promise in Hong Kong this year
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Pang Wai Hak, Pang Ka Yeung & Andy Pang Ka Wai

runners.name: Embrace Aberdeen
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 3yo who already has two 6f Sha Tin wins on his CV; raced too freely and was below form when upped to this grade there last month, but is well drawn here and may still have potential.
runners.comment: Dual winner from first five runs; below form last time (raced too freely); still respected

runners.name: Mid Winter Wind
runners.spotlight: Made the frame three consecutive times over 7f at Sha Tin in November-January, but absent since slightly disappointing run over that C&D in early February and may not be ideally suited by this drop back to 6f.
runners.comment: In frame three times over 7f in November-January; this drop back to 6f may not be ideal
runners.owner: James Lau Po Man & Alice Woo Wai See

runners.name: Causeway King
runners.spotlight: Dual 6f winner when based in New Zealand, but made a low-key Hong Kong debut here (5f) last month and has another wide draw today.
runners.comment: Made low-key Hong Kong debut here (5f) last month and has another wide draw today

runners.name: Tactical Ace
runners.spotlight: 2-5 in Australia; unable to land a significant blow on first two Hong Kong starts (both over 5f at Sha Tin) and has double-digit draw here; probably one for further down the line.
runners.comment: Safely held on first two HK starts (both 5f) and drawn wide today

runners.name: Happy Index
runners.spotlight: Placed twice at Sha Tin since clearcut win there in January, more recently when third from stall 11 last month; not yet fully exposed in Hong Kong but drawn even wider for first run at Happy Valley.
runners.comment: Placed twice since Sha Tin win in January but outside draw dents his appeal here

races.race_name: Jeju Handicap (Class 3) (Course C) (Turf)

runners.name: Spicy Gold
runners.spotlight: Four-time 6f winner; finished well down the field over that trip on recent stable debut and has left it rather late in the day to reinvent himself as a miler.
runners.comment: Out of form over 6f and has left it late in the day to reinvent himself as a miler

runners.name: Withallmyfaith
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; placed over 1m on three of his six starts since blinkers were added in November; no surprise if he gives this a good shot from stall 3.
runners.comment: Front-runner; placed over 1m on three of six starts since blinkers were added in November

runners.name: I Can
runners.spotlight: Now 3lb lower than when causing 50-1 shock over C&D in November but has looked out of sorts in recent months.
runners.comment: Shock C&D winner in November but out of sorts in recent months

runners.name: Keefy
runners.spotlight: Kept on for fourth over C&D a fortnight ago and has now made the frame four consecutive times; not ideally drawn today but has solid each-way claims nevertheless.
runners.comment: Kept on for fourth here a fortnight ago and has now made the frame four consecutive times

runners.name: Viva Graciousness
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner at start of this season but not in the same form lately; favourable draw not enough to tempt today.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner at start of this season but not in the same form lately

runners.name: Lucky Twin Stars
runners.spotlight: Came good in Hong Kong with 7f Class 4 win in January but has been unable to land a telling blow in this higher grade since (four starts) and needs to find something extra today.
runners.comment: Unable to land a telling blow in this grade since 7f Class 4 win in January

runners.name: Fantastic Fun
runners.spotlight: Triple C&D winner for new stable this season, most recently a fortnight ago; evidently in good nick but combination of move back up in grade and latest 6lb rise asks quite a lot of him today.
runners.comment: Triple C&D winner for new yard this season; rise in grade asks quite a lot of him, though
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Hamen Fan Shi Hoo, A Fan Chen Yen & C Fan Chen Mun

runners.name: Armor Golden Eagle
runners.spotlight: Made virtually all to open Hong Kong account in C&D Class 4 contest last month, his sixth attempt, and was denied a clear run when placed in this higher grade here a fortnight ago; Zac Purton takes the ride today; must be considered.
runners.comment: C&D winner last month and denied clear run when third here a fortnight ago; big player

runners.name: California Moxie
runners.spotlight: Dual C&D winner in Class 4 this season but safely held in this grade on last two appearances and has wide draw today; others look stronger.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner this season but safely held in this higher grade on last two outings

runners.name: Stormi
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; began year with hat-trick of Class 4 C&D wins in new cheekpieces and has held his form at this higher level since, but outside draw makes things awkward today.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins this year; still in good form but wide draw makes things awkward today

runners.name: Kansas
runners.spotlight: Reserve; smart and speedy 2yo when based in Ireland but launched Hong Kong career with two low-key sprint runs this month and this step up to 1m is not an obvious move.
runners.comment: Launched Hong Kong career with two low-key sprint runs this month; up in trip today

runners.name: Lady's Love
runners.spotlight: Reserve; made quite promising Hong Kong debut over 6f in February but soundly beaten over 7f since; not easy to predict on first attempt at 1m.
runners.comment: Made quite promising Hong Kong debut over 6f in February but soundly beaten over 7f since
runners.owner: HK Association Of Lady Horse Lovers Syndicate

runners.name: Solid Win
runners.spotlight: In career-best form this year, winning twice over C&D; had to settle for fourth off today's mark here last month but still enters calculations.
runners.comment: In career-best form this year, winning twice over C&D; respected

runners.name: Corleone
runners.spotlight: Progressive 5yo who has collected three C&D wins from just five starts this season; below form on latest outing eight weeks ago but may yet have more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Progressive 5yo who has collected three C&D wins from just five starts this season
